items.title: MCP generation runtime foundation — canonical registry, caller attribution, and scoped authorization
items.notes: Done 2026-07-13 (R1). One fail-fast canonical registry now owns discovery, dispatch, schema census, declared workspace scope, and per-tool error compatibility for all 13 existing families / 61 tools. All existing registered handlers retain legacy text behavior; registry-owned unknown/auth rejections are generic and non-reflective. Future tools can opt into an end-to-end sanitized/bounded json_v1 envelope, including handler-returned and thrown failures. Authenticated MCP execution carries an immutable bounded request/tool/workspace/key snapshot through explicit handler context plus a concurrency-safe compatibility bridge for existing activity writers. Full admin activity retains key attribution; client reads and shared broadcasts remove it, and admin-only key lifecycle events emit opaque invalidations. Request correlation is server-owned: the UUID used by HTTP logs/responses and durable attribution is generated internally, every caller X-Request-ID is ignored, and registry rejections exclude raw unknown tool/workspace values. Scoped auth fails closed without key ID/label, conflicting aliases remain rejected, and production dispatch census pins exact family handlers plus pre-dispatch handled-name manifests. No migration, job, AI call, product tool, endpoint, flag activation, lifecycle change, or UI ships here. Future durable generation runs must persist the explicit context snapshot rather than rely on ambient request state. Closes mcp-key-label-attribution rather than duplicating it.

items.title: Content matrix to audited page-set generation — explicit cells, deterministic preflight, durable batch, and MCP
items.notes: Replace the ambiguous keyword-only template bridge with explicit matrix/cell identity and monotonic matrix-definition/template/per-cell revisions; generation projection for one cell cannot stale siblings from the same preview. Resolve the full page block manifest, version-conditionally upgrade legacy templates, reject unsupported page types, and persist normalized run/item/attempt plus cell-evidence ledgers before one-cell brief→post closure. Typed evidence resolution requires durable source refs and fresh preview/audit; deleting placeholder text never clears a requirement. Add deterministic + typed persona/SEO/AEO/CTA item audit, one total bounded revision, post-item set audit, and a resumable cost-bounded matrix job exposed through paged MCP tools. Wire the inert Generate Brief(s) UI only after the domain/MCP path is truthful. Hard dependencies: genq-content-generation-integrity C2 and genq-content-context-v2 C3. Automatic generation stops at ready_for_human_review; explicit approval may advance to ready_to_publish; never auto-approve/send/publish. Final phase makes service/location or collection-blueprint inputs create/link a validated matrix. || [P0 CONTRACTS 2026-07-13] Shared matrix source revisions, structural-vs-ready targets, factual-source exclusions, typed evidence/placeholder policy, exact system block census, exact brief/post CAS, cell-addressed resolution, a non-empty preview-fingerprinted paid selection, operator-authorized replacement, truthful audit/run/item status, a shared 0|1 automatic-revision cap, and the reserved OFF content-matrix-generation flag are implemented. || [M0 FOUNDATION 2026-07-13] Matrix/template/per-cell CAS, deterministic structural rendering and legacy-template upgrade acceptance, bounded list/get/resolve/upgrade MCP tools, complete workspace URL collision census, and normalized run/item/attempt/evidence ledgers are implemented. Cell snapshot cursors conflict on cell-only edits; wholesale cell PUTs are rejected. Direct cell mutations use target-cell CAS and preserve history/side effects; matrix-definition regeneration uses matrix CAS and intentionally clears stale lifecycle/artifact state. The repository accepts only strict already-previewed selections and retains secret-free provenance/source identity. M0 itself opened no paid generation, preview/start route, AI call, artifact write, approval, send, or publish path. || [M1 GROUNDED ONE-CELL GENERATION 2026-07-14] Free exact-cell preview and typed evidence resolution now share HTTP and MCP adapters. Preview freezes matrix/template/cell revisions, the locked block manifest, current finalized voice, relevant approved identity, durable evidence, exact content-context fingerprint, and a bounded cost estimate before paid work. Evidence writes require factual source or artifact authority, advance only the target cell revision, and replay without duplicate side effects; unresolved facts remain typed client-input placeholders. The internal default-OFF paid seam generates brief/post candidates without premature writes, then atomically commits exact artifact revisions, cell links, and item state; late edits conflict without partial artifacts and exact completed retries replay. M1 stops at auditing_deterministic. || [M2 ITEM AUDIT + BOUNDED REVISION 2026-07-14] The internal one-item seam now runs deterministic locked-structure, URL, metadata, primary-keyword-position, internal-path, placeholder, local-evidence, CTA/AEO, thinness/duplication, and lexical voice checks; then one typed model audit for voice/persona/SEO/coherence and unsupported implications. Factual accuracy and no-hallucination remain human-required. Only an explicit model revision recommendation can spend the single allowance; the revision is post-CAS, preserves block/heading/metadata/placeholder identity, cannot add or change links, reruns deterministic checks, and receives a final model audit only when those checks pass. Attempts retain exact provider-input fingerprints and provenance; concurrent operator edits win. M2 adds no route, MCP tool, batch job, set audit, UI, review approval, send, or publication behavior. || [M3 DURABLE BATCH + MCP 2026-07-14] Explicit previewed cells now start one resumable parent job with durable per-cell checkpoints, bounded concurrency, cancellation/recovery, resource-scoped idempotency, and persisted hard call/token/cost reservations that cover every provider dispatch and survive retry. Item audits feed deterministic and named model set audits; stale or missing set verdicts cannot leave pages review-ready. Cursor-bounded reads report live counts and compact targets, and selected failed/attention items retry against exact run/item/source/artifact authority while invalidating the prior set audit. Three json_v1 MCP tools and matching authenticated HTTP adapters expose start/read/retry; accepted paid commands are metered exactly once. The existing matrix Generate Brief(s) action now performs preview, exact budget confirmation, start, progress, and selected retry behind the default-OFF flag. Human review-only approval is version-conditional and can make an individual post export-ready but never sends or publishes. || [M4 PSEO CREATION BRIDGE 2026-07-14] A collection blueprint entry plus explicit template dimensions now materializes exactly one validated, template-linked matrix through HTTP or the json_v1 create_content_matrix_from_pseo_plan MCP tool. Resource-linked replay returns the same matrix without duplicate activity; local URL collisions, duplicate targets, and exact keyword cannibalization roll back atomically. The existing structural/generation preflight remains authoritative for live-site URL census and typed service/location evidence. Creation never previews, starts generation, approves, sends, or publishes. M0-M4 are complete; the rollout flag remains default OFF pending staged activation. || [R2 CANARY READINESS 2026-07-15] Workspace-resolved Planner gating, exact MCP matrix/run deep links, and the existing revision-safe human approval action now make MCP/O1 page drafts reachable through the real browser review path without adding any publish behavior. || [R2 ONE-PAGE ROLLOUT CANARY 2026-07-15] An isolated Rinse Dental cell completed exact MCP generation, real post-editor review, and browser approval for export. The canary and independent review closed unsafe-link repair rejection, item/set human-only warning disposition, cold internal-path census handling, a partial-run candidate-census bypass and retry cycle, one-page cross-page-audit spend, the unreachable second item-revision reservation, block-crossing approved-link reuse, same-block same-URL anchor substitution, query-link entity handling, the Planner's human-only-warning approval dead-end, restart terminal misclassification, and all matching input/output/cost over-reservation. Clean initial generation reserved 14 calls / 259,483 input / 38,400 output / $1.925041 and took about 2m54s; all diagnostic retries ended at 20 calls / 376,107 input / 55,900 output / $3.033161. Approval recorded exact human evidence while Webflow IDs, publish timestamps/slugs, publish reconciliations, and content-publish jobs stayed empty. || [R2 TWO-PAGE ROLLOUT ECONOMICS 2026-07-15] A real two-cell MCP run proved that service availability plus a label was a false-readiness gap. Service targets now require separate substantive source-backed details, exact facts and source-capture freshness survive the brief-to-post adapter, final prose cannot narrate evidence mechanics, internal instructions stay out of the visible Tone & Style field, revisions receive the exact authorized placeholder census, and protocol-prefixed workspace domains no longer create malformed internal links. After one bounded revision per page, both drafts had zero deterministic failures and zero evidence-mechanics signals; conservative provenance/quality findings truthfully kept them in needs_attention. The clean run reserved 28 calls / 578,312 input / 76,800 output / $4.071766; one targeted durable re-audit brought the cumulative reservation to 30 calls / 631,088 input / 81,800 output / $4.485646. Both posts remained draft with no Webflow identity, publish timestamp/slug, reconciliation, or content-publish job. || [DIRECT TEMPLATE SURFACE 2026-07-15] MCP now lists, reads, creates, revision-updates, and duplicates reusable templates and directly materializes their explicit dimensions into matrices without a Page Strategy blueprint. The same HTTP schemas and domain functions remain authoritative; human preview, generation, review, approval, send, and publication gates are unchanged.

items.title: Brand intake to reviewed brand suite — durable evidence, voice finalization, generation/audit, MCP, and Inbox
items.source: User-directed Brand & Web Onboarding / IE pre-seed upgrade, 2026-07-13
items.notes: Persist typed immutable intake revisions and make compatibility projection idempotent. Full-suite generation first bootstraps only a provisional voice foundation from intake/authentic samples, then pauses with no running job; an operator must select authentic anchors and explicitly finalize that exact voice version before MCP resume can generate dependent identity/messaging/audience pieces. Exhaustive atomic/preset policies make voice_foundation the sole bootstrap deliverable and full_brand_system the sole bootstrap-then-resume preset; every dependent direct target/other bundle requires the exact finalized voice version. Typed evidence resolution creates a superseding intake revision, never clears from text deletion. Use named structured AI operations, CAS/provenance, hard budgets, bounded audit/revision, and naming as a creative proposal with no clearance claims. Reuse unified ClientDeliverable for grouped per-item review with honest partial state; client approval never finalizes voice. Project approved client-safe brand summary only. || [P0 CONTRACTS 2026-07-13] Typed immutable intake, singular atomic/full-suite initial-resume policy, authentic voice-sample origins, operator anchor selection/finalization, immutable approved-identity refs, human review decisions, client-safe summary, truthful bounded audit/run/item states, dormant naming vocabulary/guardrails, and the reserved OFF brand-deliverable-generation flag are implemented. Legacy paid generate and refine boundaries both reject naming before usage/AI; no reviewed naming or brand batch route is opened. || [B0 DURABLE INTAKE 2026-07-13] The existing client questionnaire, authenticated admin read/evidence mutation, and two dedicated MCP json_v1 tools now share one immutable revision service. Exact retries are actor/source-bound; evidence resolution creates or reuses a typed superseding revision with revision/idempotency checks; compatibility projection preserves calibrated voice authority and explicitly owned competitor provenance; post-commit activity, workspace broadcast, and intelligence invalidation happen once. Raw intake remains absent from client/public serializers, and the dead Brandscript label parser is retired. No AI call, brand-generation run, Inbox delivery, client approval, or voice finalization ships here, so B1-B3 remain pending. || [B1 FINALIZED VOICE 2026-07-13] Voice profiles now have monotonic revisions and immutable operator-finalized versions that freeze exact DNA, substantive guardrails, modifiers, authentic anchor content/evidence, and calibration selections into an immutable versioned snapshot. Generic edits and sample mutations reopen calibrated profiles; readiness distinguishes missing/finalized/stale authority, while legacy calibrated rows remain prompt-compatible but cannot unlock B2/M1 generation. Human HTTP finalization stays available; MCP finalization consumes a short-lived one-time operator authorization whose plaintext token is never stored, records the MCP key only as private execution provenance, and replays without phantom side effects. Site-scraped drafts no longer claim calibration, legacy voice deliverables cannot compete with calibrated Layer 2, and the brand intelligence slice carries readiness/revision/version. No paid generation, brand suite, Inbox delivery, client approval, or publication ships here, so B2-B3 remain pending. || [B2 GROUNDED BRAND GENERATION 2026-07-13] Exact immutable intake/voice/approved-brand snapshots now drive normalized, budget-bounded run/item/command/attempt ledgers through named generate/refine/audit operations. Full-suite start produces and audits only a structured provisional foundation, ends its job at the human voice-finalization gate, and resume creates the 18 durable dependents only from a later exact finalized voice. Candidate barriers, deterministic/model checkpoints, one bounded revision, final-only CAS, item-only attention/blocked output, conflict preservation, restart reconciliation, and summary-only jobs keep partial outcomes truthful. Four snake-case json_v1 MCP tools plus authenticated HTTP adapters start/read/resume/revise without exposing prompts, evidence bodies, idempotency keys, or MCP key identity. || [B3 GROUPED CLIENT REVIEW 2026-07-13] Exact B2 runs now send through the canonical ClientDeliverable/Inbox spine as separate advisory-foundation or durable-suite bundles. Per-item decisions atomically CAS source status, generation ledger/counts, and mirror state; opaque review tokens bind decisions to exactly what the client saw across stable-ID resends. Exact pending retries do not re-notify, legitimate revisions preserve terminal siblings, partial bundles may return to awaiting review, and team notifications/activity/workspace invalidations remain isolated from one another after commit. Public reads, mutation receipts, activity, and shared brand-identity events explicitly remove run/source/revision/actor lineage. The client Inbox supports independent approve/change requests, conflict/unknown-outcome recovery, accessible focus, safe prose rendering, and honest grouped status; the Brand tab reads only approved deliverables and safe voice summary with live invalidation. MCP send_to_client accepts one exact brand-generation target and never auto-approves/finalizes/publishes. B0-B3 are complete; the reserved rollout flag remains default OFF pending staged activation. || [R2 LIVE MCP CANARY 2026-07-15] A real local MCP/client-review/operator-finalization run closed GPT-5 request compatibility, prompt/schema drift, audit truncation, and invalid-candidate recovery. The final atomic dependent used the exact six-call/13,000-output-token envelope, completed one automatic revision, passed deterministic guardrails, and stopped truthfully at needs_attention for human audit warnings without artifact commit, approval, send, or publication.

items.title: Resumable MCP onboarding orchestration — intake → brand review → approved voice/identity → matrix pages
items.notes: After the independent brand and matrix lanes are staging-verified, add the shared BrandContentOnboardingRun lifecycle, durable versioned record, and MCP start/get/resume surface. The workflow pauses at voice review/finalization, operator review, client review, content authorization, and a distinct page-review gate after content generation; no generic job stays open and no human gate is bypassed. It freezes the approved brand snapshot for the page run. Automatic output stops at ready_for_human_review; every selected page needs version-safe review-only approval that satisfies export/publish preconditions but never invokes auto-publish/CMS, even when configured. Only then may awaiting_content_review advance to ready_to_publish; publication remains separate. Requires both underlying rollout flags rather than a composite flag. || [P0 CONTRACTS 2026-07-13] The shared run/status/gate vocabulary and discriminated durable gate evidence now exist, including non-empty matrix selection, operator/client content authorization proof, and page approval refs frozen to matrix run/item/cell and post revisions with a human approver. || [O1 RESUMABLE ORCHESTRATION 2026-07-14] One monotonic coordinator now references the existing brand, review, voice, matrix, and post authorities without duplicating child state. Three json_v1 MCP tools and matching authenticated HTTP start/get/resume adapters advance at most one durable gate; human content authorization remains HTTP-only. Both underlying flags, exact revisions, immutable historical command replay, pre-paid-work matrix source checks, canonical page-type identity freezing, accepted-child crash reconciliation, partial/cancelled child outcomes, all-page M3 approval evidence, and read-only publish preflight are enforced. Voice changes-requested terminalizes honestly because the foundation child has no revision path. Waiting gates keep no generic job open, and the coordinator never sends, decides, approves, or publishes. || [R2 CANARY READINESS 2026-07-15] A read-only authenticated preview now returns the exact current matrix-selection fingerprint and aggregate accepted budget required by the existing human-only authorization command; authorization recomputes the same authority immediately before paid work.

items.title: MCP chat control plane — intake submission, pSEO discovery, and voice drafting
items.notes: Adds the missing chat entry points without weakening human approval: immutable MCP intake submission with durable caller-key replay, bounded collection blueprint/entry discovery, and revision-safe voice profile/DNA/guardrail/context/sample writes. MCP-added samples remain proposed and cannot enter the authentic anchor pool until a human attests them in the platform. The follow-up added one-revision batch sample writes, pending-proposal reads, exact readiness blockers, and a unified read of every Brand & AI item awaiting a human.

items.title: Chat-driven voice review — operator attestation, platform approval, and one-time MCP code
items.source: 2026-07-15 MCP reachability dry-run and owner direction
items.notes: Makes voice approval explicit and reachable in the platform while preserving the human gate. Review & approve is the default voice section; the checklist exposes missing DNA, guardrails, authentic anchors, and MCP proposals. MCP-proposed samples require a revision-safe human attestation before they become finalization evidence. The operator may finalize directly or create the existing 15-minute, one-use, exact-command bearer code for MCP to consume. The Brand & AI top bar now exposes one pending-count modal with full voice proposals and draft deliverables plus exact visible-set batch approval; the Voice review surface adds a full-set batch proposal action. MCP cannot mint the code, attest its own samples, or use a reusable approval password.

items.title: MCP agent legibility — unified typed errors and discoverable template syntax
items.notes: Migrates the remaining 13 legacy MCP families / 61 tools to the branded json_v1 envelope, keeps field-addressed validation details, maps deterministic resource/state/precondition failures to stable codes, and prevents raw provider/AI/DB exception text from reaching agents. Template and matrix discovery now documents single-brace placeholders with /{service}-{city}. Tone direction, intake cap, and pending-approval counts were already fixed on staging. Human approval, voice-finalization, evidence, review, send, and publish gates remain unchanged.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Brand Intelligence Slice — unify voice + identity (P1–P5)
sprints.hours: TBD — phased, one PR per phase, staging-first
sprints.rationale: Terminal item history split from sprint-brand-intelligence-slice-2026-06-29. Surface a single authority-correct brand context (voice + approved identity) through the workspace intelligence facade so AI/recommendation consumers stop re-deriving it. P1 ships the read-only, behavior-neutral slice (registration + assembler + read-model extraction) behind the zero circular-dependency ratchet; P2 wires brand into the MCP content path (prepare_*_context) so agents get full voice DNA + identity — NOT a formatter/baseSlices change (server-side gen already gets full voice via buildSystemPrompt; contract #4). Plans: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-29-brand-slice-p1.md, docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-29-brand-slice-p2.md. Design: docs/designs/2026-06-26-brand-intelligence-slice.md.

items.title: Brand intelligence slice P1 — read-only, behavior-neutral registration + assembler
items.notes: Registered the `brand` slice (BrandSlice interface, INTELLIGENCE_SLICES entry, WorkspaceIntelligence.brand?, registry entry) and assembler server/intelligence/brand-slice.ts. Voice read via the unchanged buildEffectiveBrandVoiceBlock (seo-context-source.ts); identity read via a newly-extracted leaf read-model server/brand-deliverable-read-model.ts (brand-identity.ts re-exports listDeliverables/getDeliverable for back-compat). Behavior-neutral: NOT added to PROMPT_FORMATTABLE_INTELLIGENCE_SLICES, no formatters.ts case, in no baseSlices — exactly like generationQuality. Cycle-kill boundary test guards the leaf-import contract; circular-deps stays server 0/0. P2 (prompt wiring) is the next phase.

items.title: Brand intelligence slice P2 — wire brand into MCP prepare_*_context (voice DNA + identity to agents)
items.notes: Shipped the design-note P2 (MCP wiring), NOT the formatter/baseSlices path (server-side gen already gets full voice via buildSystemPrompt — adding brand to baseSlices/PROMPT_FORMATTABLE was explicitly out of scope, contract #4). (1) Extracted voiceDNAToPromptInstructions + guardrailsToPromptInstructions verbatim into a cycle-safe types-only leaf server/voice-dna-layer2.ts; prompt-assembly re-exports them (buildSystemPrompt output byte-identical). (2) Added BrandSlice.voiceDnaBlock — Layer-2 DNA+guardrails populated ONLY for calibrated profiles (non-calibrated already carry DNA in voicePromptBlock → would double-inject); brand-slice imports the renderers from the leaf, never voice-calibration.js (cycle guard). (3) prepare_brief_context/prepare_post_context fetch the brand slice separately (buildWorkspaceIntelligence(ws,{slices:['brand']})) and append ONLY voiceDnaBlock+identityPromptBlock to prompt_context (voicePromptBlock already in context.promptContext — no double-voice) plus surface structured brand_identity + voice_status payload keys. Unit+integration tests assert the brand payload, DNA/identity injection, and the Layer-2 voice header appears exactly once. circular-deps server 0/0; cycle-kill + voice-authority guards extended. Next: P3 get_brand_identity read tool; P4 consumer migration → retire shim.

items.title: Brand intelligence slice P3 — get_brand_identity MCP read tool (identity + voice status, opt-in deliverables)
items.notes: Added a read-only MCP tool get_brand_identity(workspaceId, includeDeliverables?) in a new domain-scoped server/mcp/tools/brand.ts. Reads the existing brand slice via buildWorkspaceIntelligence(ws,{slices:['brand']}) and returns { availability, identity (BrandSlice.identity verbatim — no re-derivation), voice_status, identity_prompt_block }; with includeDeliverables:true it also returns the full deliverable list (draft + approved, all types) via the P1 leaf brand-deliverable-read-model.listDeliverables for admin inspection. Identity-scoped: deliberately does NOT expose voicePromptBlock/voiceDnaBlock (voice content stays on the P2 content path). Pure DB read — no paid call, no broadcast, no activity log, no flag, no HTTP route; admin/MCP-auth gated (same posture as get_workspace_intelligence; not exposed to any client-facing endpoint). Input schema getBrandIdentityInputSchema mirrors getWorkspaceIntelligenceInputSchema's camelCase workspaceId. Lockstep registration in all three spots: server.ts ALL_TOOLS, server.ts dispatch branch, and the contract test's ALL_TOOLS array (plus the mcp-server-routing test's mocked tool set). TDD: failing unit test in mcp-tools-read-models.test.ts first (default no deliverables; includeDeliverables returns draft+approved; missing workspace/invalid args/assembly-throw → isError; no voice leakage), then implementation. circular-deps server 0/0. Next: P4 consumer migration → retire shim.

items.title: Brand intelligence slice P5 — update_brand_deliverable MCP write tool
items.notes: PR #1399. New MCP write tool update_brand_deliverable(workspaceId, deliverableId, content, expectedVersion?) in server/mcp/tools/brand.ts wrapping transactional updateDeliverableContent (snapshots prior version, bumps version, resets status→draft). Optional expectedVersion optimistic-concurrency guard mirroring update_brief; full data-flow contract (addActivity source:'mcp-chat' + broadcast BRAND_IDENTITY_UPDATED + invalidateIntelligenceCache) suppressed on no-op; update-only + workspace-scoped. handleBrandTool refactored into a name dispatcher (auto-routed via brandTools.some → no server.ts change). Real-DB test tests/unit/mcp-brand-write.test.ts (9). Adversarial review SHIP. Closes the read+write loop of the original ask.

items.title: Brand intelligence slice P4 (Option A) — lock voice-block slice-parity invariant
items.notes: PR #1400. Reframed from the planned 'retire effectiveBrandVoiceBlock': that field is NOT a shim — it is load-bearing for the prompt formatter (formatSeoContextSection receives only the seoContext slice) and its value already comes from the SAME buildEffectiveBrandVoiceBlock() as brand.voicePromptBlock, so the single source of truth already exists at the function level. Physical retirement would force brand into baseSlices (identity assembled on every prompt build) + refactor the most AI-sensitive code for zero gain. Option A (behavior-neutral): contract test tests/contract/voice-block-slice-parity.test.ts asserts the two slice fields stay byte-identical across voice states via the real buildWorkspaceIntelligence, + cross-ref JSDoc on both fields + producer-site comments. No formatter/baseSlices/consumer changes. Completes the brand-slice effort (P1–P5).

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — 🔑 Keyword Hub Health — First Paint, Memory, and Universe Read-Path Hardening
sprints.rationale: Terminal item history split from sprint-keyword-hub-health-2026-06-26. The Keyword Hub/Keyword Universe crash pattern was most likely not caused directly by circular imports; it was caused by heavy keyword/local/intelligence read paths sharing one hot graph under memory pressure. The current Hub is much safer than the original path: rows use a skinny page-bounded read path, local SEO refreshes run sequentially with heap headroom, rank evidence is capped, junk gates exist, and large local-candidate evaluation remains hard-capped. This sprint turns those patch-level protections into an explicit performance architecture: measure first, remove duplicated first-paint reads, move the remaining heavy local_candidates exception toward the skinny model, fix known facet drift, and add regression guardrails so a future universe expansion cannot quietly re-open the OOM/page-crash class.

items.title: Baseline Keyword Hub first-paint, rows, summary, and local-candidates heap profile
items.notes: DONE 2026-06-27: added the advisory `npm run report:keyword-hub-health` harness, which profiles a selected workspace through the real initial(summary+rows), split summary, split rows, and local_candidates service paths and prints timing/heap/row-count baselines for PR notes. Added/extended focused KCC tests so local_candidates remains capped even when `keyword-universe-full` is enabled, preserving the OOM boundary while the later skinny-path item remains pending.

Existing `/summary`, `/rows`, and `/detail` contracts remain intact; the client uses split-read fallbacks after initial query errors or non-initial query changes. Focused unit/integration/API tests pin initial-view parity with split summary/rows responses.

Summary, rows, detail, and initial-view services can consume the shared snapshot while retaining their own projections. Source-contract tests now pin the snapshot boundary and extend the full-model guard so summary/detail/initial cannot re-enter `buildKeywordCommandCenterModel`.

items.title: Move `local_candidates` off the full model path or prove the capped model path is the final safe boundary

The new skinny projection uses cheap `buildLocalSeoKeywordCandidates()`, prioritizes unselected candidates before selected candidates, applies `LOCAL_CANDIDATE_ROW_LIMIT`, logs truncation, preserves the `keyword-universe-full` cap boundary, and finalizes only the capped candidate slice through normal KCC row finalization.

Re-audited `candidate-boundary.ts` and confirmed the duplicated secondary-keyword junk branch called out in the audit was already gone, so no behavior change was needed there.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — 🧭 Platform Simplification — Parallel Explorer Recommendations
sprints.rationale: Terminal item history split from sprint-platform-simplification-explorers-2026-06-09. Six read-only parallel explorers audited the platform for behavior-preserving simplification opportunities: server domain shape, intelligence slices, frontend data flow, stacked feature surfaces, integration adapters, and test/guardrail leverage. This sprint captures those findings as a pick-up-ready backlog. The goal is not to remove functionality or pursue a broad rewrite; it is to make existing platform concepts more canonical by consolidating repeated lifecycle logic, cache invalidation, prompt assembly, provider transport, and guardrail patterns behind the conventions the codebase already uses.

items.title: Feature stack — consolidate client-facing insight projections into a read-only view model
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `src/components/client/Briefing/InsightsBriefingPage.tsx`, `src/components/client/InsightsEngine.tsx`, `src/components/client/AnalyticsTab.tsx`, `src/hooks/client/useClientBriefing.ts`, `src/hooks/client/useClientInsights.ts`, `src/hooks/client/useClientIntelligence.ts`, `src/hooks/useRecommendations.ts`, `server/routes/public-analytics.ts`, `server/routes/client-intelligence.ts`, `server/routes/recommendations.ts`, `server/briefing-client-projection.ts`, `server/insight-narrative.ts`, `server/monthly-digest.ts`. User-facing behavior to preserve: briefing magazine, monthly digest, recommendations, narrative insights, raw Analytics tab, Strategy deep links, and tier gating. Current complexity: client insight data is split across public intelligence, insight narrative, digest, briefing, recommendations, and raw GA4/GSC endpoints; components recompose overlapping summaries independently. Behavior-preserving path: introduce a read-only client insight view-model assembler returning named sections such as briefing, pulse, recommendations, narrative, digest, and intelligence summary. Keep existing endpoints during migration; raw Analytics charts stay separate. Tests/guardrails: client intelligence routes, briefing public tests, public analytics tests, hook/component coverage for briefing page, and public serialization tests proving admin-only fields remain scrubbed. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P2. Framing is still correct. Re-scope note: verify it stays behavior-preserving (keep existing endpoints + raw Analytics charts separate, per its own notes). This is a real-but-discretionary simplification, not a bug — sequence it after higher-impact client work, and pair it with the client IA redesign if/when that lands so the assembler matches the final tab shape. || PARTIAL 2026-06-26: extracted the `/api/public/intelligence/:workspaceId` tier slice selection and client-safe response projection into `server/client-insight-view-model.ts`; route now delegates as an HTTP adapter while preserving response shape, admin-only insight scrubbing, G3 pre-cap insight counts, tier-gated fields, keyword feedback sample caps, copy pipeline summary, and premium site-health/decay projections. Added pure view-model tests and a source-boundary contract. || PARTIAL 2026-06-26: moved client narrative insight projection ownership behind client insight view-model leaf modules, added client briefing and digest view entrypoints, rewired public narrative/digest and public/admin briefing preview routes to import through those leaf boundaries, and left compatibility exports for existing callers. || PARTIAL 2026-06-26: extracted public recommendation set/single-rec/response-summary projections into `server/client-insight-recommendation-view-model.ts`, keeping recommendation route mutations as HTTP orchestration while preserving public response shapes, The Issue flag gating, EMV stripping, operator wording/order projection, and response summary caps. Remaining scope: fold broader named dashboard sections into the shared read model without changing existing endpoints or raw Analytics charts. || DONE 2026-06-26: completed the client insight read-model consolidation by adding `src/hooks/client/useClientInsightViewModel.ts` as the client-side owner for narrative insights, monthly digest, published briefing, public intelligence, and public recommendation-set query contracts. Legacy hooks now delegate as compatibility wrappers, `InsightsEngine` reads recommendations through the shared section hook, and source-boundary tests prevent per-component query ownership from drifting back while raw Analytics charts remain separate as intended.

items.title: Feature stack — extract content pipeline workflow mutations into focused hooks/services
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx`, `src/components/ContentBriefs.tsx`, `src/components/ContentManager.tsx`, `src/hooks/admin/useAdminBriefs.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/useAdminPosts.ts`, `src/api/content.ts`, `server/routes/content-briefs.ts`, `server/routes/content-requests.ts`, `server/routes/content-posts.ts`, `server/domains/content/send-post-to-client.ts`, `server/content-brief.ts`, `server/content-posts.ts`, `server/content-requests.ts`. User-facing behavior to preserve: planner/calendar/briefs/posts/subscriptions tabs, request-to-brief generation, post generation, send to client, client review, Webflow publish, and exports. Current complexity: large components still mix workflow mutation logic, cache writes, raw API calls, local state machines, and rendering. Behavior-preserving path: create focused hooks such as `useAdminBriefWorkflow`, `useAdminContentRequestWorkflow`, and `useAdminPostWorkflow` over `src/api/content.ts`; keep components as render/state shells. Server route growth should stop; reusable transitions move into content-domain services. Tests/guardrails: ContentBriefs component tests, ContentManager send-to-client tests, content brief/post/request route tests, status transition tests, and public post review broadcast tests. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Re-scope and keep, P2. The data-fetching layer is mostly extracted; the remaining work is consolidating the workflow MUTATION + local-state-machine logic out of ContentManager/ContentBriefs into the named workflow hooks. Reframe the item to 'extract remaining post/brief workflow state machines into useAdminPostWorkflow/useAdminBriefWorkflow' rather than full from-scratch extraction, since the API hooks already exist. || PHASE 1 DONE 2026-06-26: extracted `useAdminPostWorkflow` for ContentManager post list/query state, `?post=` deep-link receiver, status update, publish, delete, send-to-client inline note state, voice-score job polling, status counts, and filter/sort helpers. Added focused unit/source-contract coverage and reran existing ContentManager component coverage. || DONE 2026-06-26: extracted `useAdminBriefWorkflow` for ContentBriefs data loading, generator form state, fixContext prefill/auto-generate, background job watchers, request status/delivery mutations, delete/send/export/copy actions, regeneration jobs, post generation, and PostEditor open/close state. Added pure-helper tests plus source-contract coverage that keeps raw API/cache/job workflow code out of `ContentBriefs`. No additional deferred recommendation was identified; `ContentPipeline` remains a tab/prefill shell rather than a mutation workflow owner.

Added `extractGeneratedBriefResult()` and `renderBriefMarkdown()` helper coverage plus source-contract tests for the extraction boundary. No route/API behavior changed.

items.title: Feature stack — centralize keyword opportunity display projection without merging scoring layers
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `server/scoring/keyword-value-score.ts`, `server/scoring/opportunity-value.ts`, `server/keyword-command-center.ts`, `server/keyword-strategy-enrichment.ts`, `server/keyword-recommendations.ts`, `server/briefing-client-projection.ts`, `src/components/client/strategy/StrategyKeywordDrawer.tsx`, `src/components/keyword-command-center/KeywordDetailDrawer.tsx`, `src/components/client/strategy/StrategyContentOpportunitiesSection.tsx`. User-facing behavior to preserve: Layer 1 Hub keyword value, Layer 2 recommendation opportunity value, client-safe explanations, content-gap ordering, and brief/request flows. Current complexity: scoring layers are intentionally separate, but display/ranking projections are scattered; surfaces consume `valueScore`, `opportunityScore`, fallback ordering, and 'why this matters' explanations differently. Behavior-preserving path: do not merge scoring algorithms. Add a shared projection helper for keyword-bearing opportunities that returns normalized keyword, display score, reasons, dollar value if allowed, client-safe explanation, and sort key. Use behind existing flags where ordering changes. Tests/guardrails: keyword value score tests, opportunity value tests, content gap scoring tests, keyword recommendation ranking tests, client strategy content gap tests, briefing preview tests, and flag-off parity tests. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P2. Framing is still correct — the 'do not merge scoring algorithms; add a shared display-projection helper returning normalized keyword + display score + reasons + client-safe explanation + sort key' approach is sound and the duplicate display helpers confirm the need. The duplicated `opportunityScore()` between pageIntelligenceDisplay.ts and the server scoring is the concrete consolidation target. || DONE 2026-06-26: Added `shared/keyword-opportunity-projection.ts` as the display/sort projection helper without importing or merging score-authority modules. Migrated briefing recommendations, strategy enrichment ordering, intelligence prompt content-gap ordering, client strategy keyword ordering, client content-gap backfilled ordering, and admin content-gap score/volume/priority ordering onto the shared helper. Added unit coverage for projection/comparator parity plus a source-contract guard preventing migrated surfaces from drifting back to local opportunity-score sort snippets.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Generation Quality & Performance — integrity before experimentation

The program protects operator edits and lifecycle state first, repairs authoritative evidence, removes avoidable read/token/provider work, then adds runtime measurement before conversion-grounded and client-evidence roadmap companions. Audit: docs/superpowers/audits/2026-07-13-generation-quality-performance-preplan-audit.md. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-13-generation-quality-performance-program-spec.md.
