items.title: AI execution governance — operation cache policy, provider-neutral run telemetry, and provenance contracts
items.notes: Done 2026-07-13. Named-operation none/inflight/ttl policy now governs dispatcher-backed OpenAI and Anthropic text calls; signal-bearing calls remain uncached and cancellation-isolated. Execution metadata, provider attempts, token usage, operation traces, cache outcomes, origin-run reuse, and real Claude-to-GPT fallback chains share truthful correlation IDs without storing prompts. Cache statistics use cumulative events and real time windows, live pending calls are never age-evicted into duplicate provider work, and the typed GenerationProvenance contract is ready for durable consumers. The unused typed Anthropic tool helper remains explicitly cache-none and outside dispatcher governance until a production caller is introduced.

items.title: Keyword evidence integrity — preserve stored intent, CPC, source, and provenance through scoring
items.notes: Done 2026-07-13. Stored search intent now survives the lite page adapter into real KCC routes. CPC, intent, membership source, and field-level evidence provenance survive canonical/fallback pools, incremental competitor cache, assignment, every enrichment writer, page/content-gap persistence, sanitizer replacement, and the recommendation Opportunity Value path; unusable CPC remains unknown instead of authoritative zero. Keyword strategy generation now stores an exact effective-input fingerprint plus provider-neutral run provenance, uses a monotonic revision, and commits with transactional CAS so every audited feedback/vote/managed-set/tracked-keyword/business-priority/operator edit wins without repeating provider work. Internal provenance is omitted from workspace/public serialization. Migrations 179-180.

items.title: Content generation integrity — truthful completion, strict artifacts, edit-safe saves, and provenance
items.notes: Done 2026-07-14. C1 enforces strict complete artifacts, truthful needs_attention/error outcomes, one bounded copy repair, and delivery gates. C2 adds monotonic revisions, durable provider-neutral provenance, version-conditional generation and human/lifecycle mutations, exact MCP prepared-context authority, resource-scoped job claims, serialized autosaves, public redaction, Webflow reconciliation, and guarded post-commit effects. C2 merged to staging through PR #1537 with required CI and post-merge checks green, unlocking content context v2 and paid matrix generation.

items.title: Keyword Command Center read performance — first-paint hydration and a true skinny projection
items.notes: Complete 2026-07-13. First paint now uses /initial exactly once to seed independent canonical summary and row caches; subsequent interactions use /rows without re-running full hydration. KCC reads use a context-owned skinny projection instead of the full strategy assembler, rank freshness is explicit, local-candidate counts preserve market-identity parity, and production query-count and p95 budgets are registered and pinned with real SQL execution instrumentation. This closes the remaining keyword-hub-wave4 read-path work.

items.title: Content generation context v2 — budgeted evidence projection and exact-once voice authority
items.notes: Done 2026-07-14 behind the default-OFF, workspace-aware content-generation-context-v2 flag. One intelligence build returns bounded brief, draft, and voiceReview projections plus one captured system-prompt authority; initial brief generation, staged post generation and section regeneration, voice scoring, and MCP brief/post preparation consume those projections without a second brand/context read. The brief projection carries only the matching page-map row, timestamped external SERP/reference/style evidence is sanitized as untrusted data, and missing metrics/SERP facts remain unknown/needs_research. A representative 20-row matrix reduces the page-map projection from 723 to 57 estimated tokens (92%); deterministic quality fixtures and legacy flag-OFF suites pass. Staging runtime p95/quality smoke remains the promotion gate before enabling or retiring the flag.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Reconcile migration (R0–R12) — data/type-layer cracks before the P2 rebuild
sprints.rationale: Terminal item history split from sprint-reconcile-migration-2026-07-01. Close the verified data/type-layer cracks (honest Prove ledger, single-authority state, enforced vocabulary, observable execution, safe migrations) so the P2 UI rebuild lands on ground that does not move. Zero client-visible changes in this phase. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-reconcile-migration.md. || CLOSEOUT 2026-07-02 (C4): all 24 tickets on STAGING (Wave A promoted to main #1443; Waves B+C #1444–#1463). Exit criteria green on merged staging (verify:platform all 14 steps, verify:lexicon, 28,019 tests, pr-check). Whole-arc holistic review (5 Fable reviewers, e5745db2b..staging) found 19 cross-ticket integration defects — ALL FIXED in the C4 remediation (5 themes A–E: attribution honesty at every client seam; struck≠completed payload safety incl. migration 171; recommendation mirror/respond integrity; snapshot/archive/sweep infra incl. migration 172 twin indexes + C1 workspace_id-written-forward; job owner-scope + pr-check/GLOSSARY hygiene). ~150 tests added; full-suite gate green. See FEATURE_AUDIT #612. staging→main PRODUCTION promotion HELD for owner. Follow-ups queued: external/manual outcome ingestion; full flag-sunset + dead-code strip before the P2 rebuild.

items.title: R1-PR1 — Lexicon registry + GLOSSARY restructure (root vocabulary contract)
items.notes: Promoted GLOSSARY.md from a flat reference into an enforced contract. New machine-readable registry shared/types/lexicon.ts (LexiconEntry with wordClass canonical|externally-mirrored|historical|proposed + DUPLICATE_NAME_ALLOWLIST seeded from the verified 30-name duplicate-export census: Deliverable* → R2, GA4/GSC/ROI mirror + server-internal twins → permanent). verify:lexicon (scripts/lexicon-registry.ts) checks GLOSSARY↔registry parity both directions + a live duplicate-exported-name scan vs the allowlist + allowlist hygiene; wired pr-ci-blocking into package.json, ci.yml, verify-platform.ts, and the governance registry. GLOSSARY restructured into the 4 word-class sections + PROPOSED intake of the untracked redesign-mockup vocabulary (thread kinds request/instruction/approval, promotable, thread new/ack/handled, 4 cockpit rails, promote-to-strategy-signal). docs/rules/lexicon.md contract doc; CLAUDE.md (Key Docs + Code Conventions + Quality Gates + Commands) + FEATURE_AUDIT #541 updated. TDD: tests/unit/lexicon-registry.test.ts (11 tests). A3 (pr-check duplicate-name + ActivityType minting rules) is the follow-up; R2 burns the two Deliverable* entries off the allowlist.

items.title: R10-PR1 — Cron registry + stop hooks (single execution surface for boot-wired schedulers)
items.notes: New server/cron-registry.ts: CRON_METADATA (Record<CronId, {label; module; intervalMs; description; stopHook; start; stop; exemptReason?}>) mirroring shared/types/background-jobs.ts's BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA shape. Lazy construction — the module's top-level scope never calls start()/stop(); only startAllRegisteredCrons()/stopAllRegisteredCrons() (called from server/startup.ts and server/index.ts gracefulShutdown respectively) invoke lifecycle hooks. Registers all 19 boot-wired scheduler subsystems (email-throttle, scheduled-audits, approval-reminders, monthly-reports, backup, trial-reminders, churn-signals, anomaly-detection, outcome-crons, data-retention, intelligence-cache-refresh, competitor-monitoring, insight-recompute, rank-tracking, ga4-conversion-snapshot, webflow-form-poller, briefing-cron, strategy-issue-cron, return-hook-cron) plus 3 known module-level timers as documented stopHook:false exemptions (server/middleware.ts rate-limit + login-lockout cleanup, server/ai-deduplication.ts dedup cache cleanup — unconditional import-time timers with no existing stop export; restructuring deferred, not silently dropped) and the MCP TTL sweeper (server/mcp/handles.ts, already NODE_ENV=test guarded) similarly exempt. Closed all 9 stop-hook gaps the R10 inventory found: added stopBackupScheduler (backup.ts had none) and wired 4 previously-orphaned stop exports (ga4-conversion-snapshot, webflow-form-poller, strategy-issue-cron, return-hook-cron) into gracefulShutdown via the single stopAllRegisteredCrons() call — replacing the old hand-listed 14-call block. server/startup.ts now delegates to startAllRegisteredCrons() instead of 19 hand-listed imports (initEmailQueue + clearTestModeCustomerIds remain hand-called — one-shot, not recurring crons). New tests/unit/cron-registry.test.ts (metadata completeness, exemptReason required when stopHook:false, lazy-construction guard, start/stop-all helpers) and tests/contract/cron-registry-census.test.ts (anti-drift census: every boot-wired module + known module-level timer has a registry entry; gracefulShutdown/startSchedulers wired through the registry helpers). Simplified tests/unit/startup.test.ts from mocking 15-of-20 scheduler imports (the historical hazard — 5 unmocked real modules could start real timers inside vitest) down to mocking the 3 modules startup.ts now actually imports (email.js, stripe.js, cron-registry.js). Deferred per owner decision: throttledUntil-to-cron conversion DROPPED (not implemented); strategy-issue-cron.ts and outcome-crons.ts are registered as metadata entries but their internal construction/execution-surface adoption (createIntervalCron) is NOT refactored — deferred to a follow-up after B6 (measured R4×R10 file collision); the 16 hand-rolled interval modules are not migrated onto the shared createIntervalCron primitive — that consolidation is R10-PR2. pr-check rule ('new setInterval must register in CRON_METADATA') evaluated and explicitly DEFERRED — documented in docs/rules/background-generation.md's new §Cron Registry: a regex/customCheck rule cannot reliably distinguish a boot-wired module-level scheduler from a request-scoped setInterval (e.g. the SSE keepalive in server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts) without real scope analysis. Honest scope of the census contract test: it catches an already-inventoried scheduler LOSING its CRON_METADATA entry, cron-registry.ts dropping an inventory import, gracefulShutdown/startSchedulers no longer routing through the registry helpers, and startup.ts bypassing the registry via a direct scheduler import — but it does NOT auto-detect a brand-new scheduler absent from its hardcoded inventory (it iterates that list, not startup.ts's live imports), so adding a new scheduler is a manual two-step: register in cron-registry.ts AND extend the census inventory. Auto-detecting an un-inventoried new scheduler is what the deferred pr-check rule would add. The R10 inventory's claimed background-jobs-lifecycle.test.ts gap (missing 409 non-cancellable HTTP pin) was found to already exist at tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts:89 — not duplicated. docs/rules/background-generation.md + CLAUDE.md pointer updated same-commit. Verified: npm run typecheck clean, npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts zero errors, targeted vitest (cron-registry.test.ts 7 + cron-registry-census.test.ts 7 + startup.test.ts 1) 15/15 green.

items.title: R10-PR2 — Cancel-gate pin + system-job class (Lane I final ticket)
items.notes: Re-verified the ratified 'job-cancel route must consult BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA[type].cancellable' deliverable: ALREADY implemented at HEAD (server/routes/jobs.ts:193 getJobCancellationError, landed 914b68f19) with an existing HTTP 409 integration test at tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts:89 — no re-implementation, no duplicate test added. Real remaining work: system-job class. Added BackgroundJobClass ('user'|'system') + class field to every BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA entry + isSystemJobType(type) predicate in shared/types/background-jobs.ts, keyed by job TYPE (not a DB column — zero migrations, jobs table untouched, works retroactively on already-persisted rows). INTELLIGENCE_RECOMPUTE is the sole 'system' entry; all other 37 types are 'user'. It is created exclusively through server/intelligence-recompute-job.ts's enqueueIntelligenceRecompute (flag-gated behind signal-auto-recompute, hasActiveJob-deduped), called from THREE producer paths so removing any single caller does NOT retire the type: (1) server/insight-recompute-cron.ts (daily activity-gated cron), (2) server/rank-tracking-scheduler.ts (daily rank-tracking cron, refreshing signals after new rank snapshots land), (3) server/keyword-strategy-follow-ons.ts (on-mutation follow-on after a strategy/content update settles — not a cron). Because class is a property of the job TYPE, all three paths are gated identically; which caller enqueued it is irrelevant to client visibility. Wired isSystemJobType as a second, independent gate (always wins on top of the existing CLIENT_VISIBLE_JOB_TYPES allow-list, so a type mistakenly allow-listed in future still can't leak) into both client-visibility surfaces: server/routes/jobs.ts isClientVisibleJob() (GET /api/public/jobs, feeds AgencyWorkFeed.tsx via useClientWorkFeed.ts) and server/websocket.ts resolveJobDelivery() (unauthenticated WS delivery path for JOB_CREATED/JOB_UPDATED). Admin surfaces (GET /api/jobs, authenticated WS path, NotificationBell.tsx, useBackgroundTasks.tsx) were already unfiltered by type and required no change — 'admin shows everything' was already true by construction. No WS payload/schema change (class is derived client-side from job.type via the shared metadata table, exactly like cancellable already is), so no contract-test/handler updates were needed beyond the new pinning assertions themselves — JOB_CREATED/JOB_UPDATED stay in ws-invalidation-coverage's LOCAL_ONLY_EVENTS unchanged. TDD: extended tests/unit/background-jobs.test.ts + tests/unit/shared-types-validation.test.ts (isSystemJobType classification + safe unknown-type default), tests/unit/websocket-job-delivery.test.ts (system job reaches admin, blocked from unauthenticated client even hypothetically allow-listed), tests/integration/agency-work-feed.test.ts (system job absent from GET /api/public/jobs, present via GET /api/jobs). docs/rules/background-generation.md new §System Job Class documents the contract + narrowest-filter-point rationale. Verified: npm run typecheck clean, npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts zero errors, targeted vitest 15 files / 418 tests green (background-jobs, shared-types-validation, websocket-job-delivery, agency-work-feed, jobs-routes, background-jobs-lifecycle, jobs-cancellation, mcp-tools-job-actions, mcp-tools-jobs, background-job-coverage-contract, background-job-task-panel-wiring, mcp-tool-job-name-lockstep, useBackgroundTasks.realtime, ws-invalidation-coverage, websocket-event-shapes). Lane I (R10) complete.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Design cleanup — operator + client surface hierarchy & system consolidation
sprints.rationale: Terminal item history split from sprint-design-cleanup-2026-06-30. From the 2026-06-30 UX reviews of three surfaces judged against the design system primitives and the four laws of color: WorkspaceOverview (agency Command Center), WorkspaceHome (single-workspace operator home), and TheIssueClientPage (the verdict-first client money surface, flag the-issue-client-spine). Shared root cause on the operator screens: everything is surfaced at equal weight, so the eye has nowhere to land. The client surface is already strong — its items are polish + lifting recurring patterns into shared components. All fixes reuse existing primitives. Full findings index: design-system/guidelines/ux-findings-index.html. Per-screen artifacts: ux-review-command-center.html, ux-review-workspace-home.html, ux-review-client-issue.html. Item id prefixes map to screen: cc-=Command Center, wh-=Workspace Home, issue-=The Issue client, x-=cross-cutting. Canonical spec + per-item files/dependsOn/acceptanceCriteria/verify: docs/design-cleanup/design-cleanup-sprint.json. Working agreement: docs/design-cleanup/design-cleanup-working-agreement.md. Build plan: docs/design-cleanup/design-cleanup-sprint-brief.html.

items.title: Command Center header — collapse 5 colored buttons to one neutral cluster + one primary
items.notes: WorkspaceOverview header renders Prospect/Roadmap/AI Usage/Revenue/Features as five equal secondary buttons, three individually colored amber/emerald/teal — spending the status palette on navigation (teal 'Features' reads as the primary CTA, amber 'AI Usage' reads as a warning). Fix: demote the five to one neutral secondary cluster or an overflow 'More' menu, promote the actual entry action (new/onboard workspace) to the single mint primary, and strip amber/emerald/teal off chrome. Reserve those hues for status only. [Wave 1: shipped]

items.title: Command Center — promote Needs Attention to the top with real presence
items.notes: Needs Attention is the prioritized, deep-linked worklist — the reason operators open the screen — but it sits mid-page at default weight below the header. Fix: make it the first block under PageHeader; add a count in the title ('Needs Attention · 6'); add a subtle red/amber left-accent when a P1 (churn/new request) exists; keep the chevron always visible so rows read as actionable. Component already exists (SectionCard + ClickableRow); this is ordering + emphasis only. [Wave 1: shipped]

items.title: Command Center — one headline metric, drop stats duplicated by Needs Attention
items.notes: Global stat row shows six equal StatCards; nothing leads, and New Requests / Approvals / Content duplicate the Needs Attention list directly above. Fix: render one headline (Hours Saved — the value story) at size=hero spanning two columns, keep only at-a-glance trend metrics (Avg Health, Active, Content), and drop the actionable counts that already live in Needs Attention. Reduces the row from 6 to ~4 with a clear lead. [Wave 1: shipped]

items.title: Command Center — workspace rows lead with score + single rollup, details on demand
items.notes: Each workspace row packs five metric columns (Health/Requests/Approvals/Content/SEO Status) with inline colored micro-text in every cell; at 3+ workspaces it becomes a wall of multicolored numbers and color loses meaning. Fix: lead each row with the ranking number (health score, in band color) + a single 'N need you' rollup pill; move the five-column breakdown into the row's hover/expanded state or the workspace detail. Color only on the rollup; neutral text elsewhere. [Wave 1: shipped]

items.title: Color-law sweep — stop using mint (text-accent-brand) on static data
items.notes: Static counts are rendered in text-accent-brand (mint) across the overview ('3 active', '5 delivered', approvals count). Mint is the action color; on static numbers it makes everything read as clickable and dilutes real CTAs. Fix: recolor static counts to blue (data) or emerald (completed/positive); mint only on actual click targets. Do as a repo-wide grep for text-accent-brand / var(--teal) on non-interactive text — complements the existing report-style-drift.ts baseline. Highest-leverage, lowest-risk item. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome follow-on — tame the up-to-nine equal hero StatCards
items.notes: WorkspaceHome (WH-F1) renders up to nine StatCards all at size=hero (Site Health, Search Clicks, Users, Rank Changes, Traffic Value, Content Decay, Content Pipeline, Content Velocity, Coverage Gaps) — a sharper version of CC-F2 where nine hero metrics means no hero. Fix: choose 2-3 hero metrics, demote the rest to a compact size=default secondary rail (still present, still deep-linked). Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome — collapse four health representations into one canonical block
items.notes: Site Health appears four times on one screen: WorkspaceHealthBadge (under header), the Site Health StatCard, the MetricRing trailing inside that card, and WorkspaceHealthBar. One number, four widgets. Fix: keep the StatCard + trailing MetricRing as the single canonical health block; drop the standalone badge; fold HealthBar's setup% into the onboarding consolidation (design-wh-setup-triplicated). Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome — recolor Users metric off purple; tokenize hardcoded iconColors
items.notes: The Users StatCard sets iconColor='#a78bfa' (purple) — a direct violation of the fourth color law (purple = admin AI only, never client-facing data). Several sibling cards also hardcode hex (#22d3ee, #4ade80, #f87171) instead of tokens. Fix: recolor Users to var(--blue) (it's a data metric); replace every hardcoded hex iconColor with a token so light mode + palette shifts stay correct; keep purple exclusive to AdminChat/admin intelligence. Pairs with the repo-wide color sweep. Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: Color sweep — tokenize repo-wide hardcoded hex iconColors/SVG/inline colors to CHART_SERIES_COLORS/tokens
items.notes: ~50+ hardcoded hex instances beyond the 3 Law-4 purple cards (RedirectManager, SiteArchitecture, LocalSeoVisibilityPanel, GbpAuthenticatedReviewsPanel, OutcomeScorecard, matrix/CellDetailPanel, client charts, audit/*, etc.) map to existing CHART_SERIES_COLORS constants or tokens. Keep brand-justified (#059669 OpenAI, #ea580c Anthropic, chart-hex-ok) and CSS-var fallbacks. Add missing constants to src/components/ui/constants.ts. Wave 0b. See docs/superpowers/audits/2026-06-30-design-cleanup-audit.md §1b. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome — show setup tasks in one place, not three
items.notes: Link Webflow / connect GSC / connect GA4 appears simultaneously in the OnboardingChecklist (top), WorkspaceHealthBar recommendations, and Needs Attention 'setup suggestions'. Same three tasks rendered three times for a half-configured workspace. Fix: give setup tasks one owner (OnboardingChecklist while unconfigured); once dismissed/complete, HealthBar + Needs Attention surface operational follow-ups only. Never render the same connect-task in two components at once. Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — extract one shared NeedsAttention/AttentionRow component
items.notes: Command Center and Workspace Home both render a 'Needs Attention' worklist but with different layouts: Command Center uses dot + Badge value + workspace name + chevron; Workspace Home uses colored icon + label + sub + ArrowUpRight plus a collapsible 'setup suggestions' sub-group. Same job, two visual languages. Fix: extract one AttentionRow / NeedsAttention component into the flow/ group with a single severity-color mapping and interaction; both screens consume it. Closes the divergence for good. Artifacts: ux-review-command-center.html (F3), ux-review-workspace-home.html (F5). [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: WorkspaceHome — lead with the worklist; consider tabbing the lower sections
items.notes: ~10 stacked full-width sections; the most actionable block (Needs Attention) lands below nine metric cards + the health bar, well under the fold. Fix: order as worklist (after onboarding for new workspaces) -> trimmed metric grid -> operational sections; consider grouping the lower half under the existing TabBar (Overview/Pipeline/Activity) so the default view isn't a ten-section scroll. Depends on design-wh-statcard-hierarchy + design-x-needs-attention-shared. Artifact: ux-review-workspace-home.html. [Wave 2: shipped]

items.title: The Issue (client) — bring the Content Plan up directly under the verdict
items.notes: On the spine branch the Content Plan (the only retainer action, 'Request this') sits at slot 5, beneath three stacked proof surfaces (verdict $, outcome count, money/ROI frame) plus the loop footer. A client convinced by the verdict may never scroll to act. Fix: keep verdict as the lead, then surface Content Plan directly under it (verdict -> plan -> proof), with outcome count + money + wins following. Strong A/B candidate on request-rate. Note: the flag-OFF branch already leads with the plan. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: The Issue (client) — render ROIDashboard once, not twice
items.notes: Slot 3 mounts <ROIDashboard compact /> and 'Under the hood' mounts <ROIDashboard compact={false} /> again — the same dollar story twice on one page (possible double fetch, 'which number is right?' confusion). Fix: render ROI once; keep the compact money frame at slot 3 and let 'Under the hood' hold only methodology/tables, or make the compact frame expand in place. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: The Issue (client) — relate the top 'Your turn' strip to the 'Ask your strategist' loop
items.notes: ActionQueueStrip (slot 0, 'Your turn') and IssueLoopFooter (slot 4, 'the full pending-decisions queue/loop') are two pending-work surfaces bookending the page; a client may read the footer as new work. Fix: make the top strip the count/jump ('3 decisions waiting v') that anchors to the footer detail, sharing one visual language. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — define one section-header treatment in the design system
items.notes: On The Issue, a top-level section header is variously a muted h2.t-label kicker, a SectionCard title bar, or a <summary> row — three weights for one job, making a long scroll hard to skim. Fix: standardize one section-header treatment (the t-label kicker) for top-level sections and reserve SectionCard headers for cards within a section. Applies app-wide. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Closeout: shipped]

items.title: Content Pipeline — unify the WorkflowStepper and tab bar into one journey
items.notes: Two navigations describe the same flow differently: the WorkflowStepper says Strategy->Briefs->Posts->Publish while the tab bar says Planner/Calendar/Briefs/Posts/Subscriptions. 'Strategy' leaves the page, 'Publish' and 'Subscriptions' are the same destination under two names, and Planner/Calendar exist only in tabs. Fix: align stepper phases 1:1 with tabs and labels (rename Subscriptions->Publish; point stepper 'Strategy' at the Planner tab, not off-page); treat Calendar as a view toggle, not a peer phase. Stepper shows where you are; tabs are how you move. Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 4: shipped]

items.title: Content Pipeline — replace the hand-rolled tab bar with the TabBar primitive
items.notes: The sub-tab bar is built inline from <Button variant='ghost'> with a manual border-b-2 mint underline — re-implementing the TabBar component the system ships and WorkspaceHome already uses. They will drift in padding, hit-target, and keyboard nav. Fix: adopt the TabBar primitive (supports icons + mint active state + arrow-key nav + 44px hit target); move Export beside it as an action, not a fake tab. Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 4: shipped]

items.title: Content Pipeline — roll up four pre-content alert bands
items.notes: Between the stepper and tab content sit four full-width bands: health summary, content-decay alert, cannibalization warnings, AI-suggested briefs — three of them amber/red, so the page reads as alarmed before any action. Fix: keep the neutral health summary as the one persistent bar; roll decay + cannibalization + suggestions into a single collapsible 'Alerts & suggestions (N)' strip, or move each into the tab it acts on (decay->Posts, suggestions->Briefs). Reach the work in one screen. Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 4: shipped]

items.title: Content Pipeline — neutralize mint/amber on static counts in the health bar
items.notes: The health summary renders brief/matrix counts in text-accent-brand (mint) and the posts count in text-accent-warning (amber) — static data wearing action/warning colors (same drift as CC-F5; amber implies a problem that isn't there). Fix: counts are data -> neutral bright (or blue); reserve amber for genuine decay/cannibalization warnings. Folds into the repo-wide color-law sweep (design-color-law-sweep-mint-on-data). Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — add a Menu/Dropdown primitive to the design system
items.notes: The Content Pipeline Export control hand-rolls its own open state, click-outside handler, positioned panel, and per-row CSV/JSON buttons — and the design system has no Dropdown/Menu primitive, so the next menu gets hand-rolled differently. This is the first genuine NEW-component need surfaced by the review program. Fix: add a Menu/Dropdown primitive (trigger + positioned panel + click-outside + keyboard dismiss) and adopt it here. Artifact: ux-review-content-pipeline.html. [Wave 4: shipped]

items.title: The Issue cockpit — keep Send issue in reach of the staging queue
items.notes: Send issue lives in IssueHeader at the top (canSend = stagedCount > 0), but the operator builds the staged set in the BackingMovesQueue near the bottom of the spine. Workflow becomes stage-at-bottom -> scroll-up-to-send, with the consuming button off-screen while staging. Fix: add a sticky send bar (or a Send affordance docked to BackingMovesQueue) that appears once stagedCount > 0, showing the staged count + Send from where staging happens; keep the header Send too. Artifact: ux-review-admin-cockpit.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — define one Disclosure/collapsible-section pattern; split the catch-all drawers
items.source: UX review 2026-06-30 · AC-F2 (mirrors client 'Under the hood')
items.notes: The cockpit's 'Supporting detail' <details> holds eight unrelated surfaces (leads, keyword-targets link, content-work-order lens, trust ladder, orient, content gaps, cannibalization, strategy diff) behind one all-or-nothing toggle. The client TheIssueClientPage 'Under the hood' does the same (ring + full ROI + leads + competitors). Fix: split each into 2-3 labeled disclosures grouped by job, AND define a single Disclosure/collapsible-section pattern in the design system (consistent summary chrome + behavior + sub-grouping) consumed by both screens. Retires AC-F2 and the client under-the-hood catch-all. Artifact: ux-review-admin-cockpit.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: The Issue cockpit — tokenize the POV-staleness nudge (raw amber)
items.notes: The 'POV may be out of date' banner uses raw Tailwind text-amber-400 / border-amber-500/30 / bg-amber-500/5 instead of the --amber token + the soft-warning surface convention (StatusBadge/InlineBanner). Won't follow palette/light-theme changes; a second way of spelling 'warning surface'. Fix: use the warning tokens + shared soft-amber convention. Pairs with WH-F3 / the color-law + tokenize-hardcoded-hex work. Artifact: ux-review-admin-cockpit.html. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: The Issue cockpit — dock 'Add a recommendation' to the BackingMovesQueue header
items.notes: A right-aligned 'Add a recommendation' button sits on its own line between the Drafted POV editor and the BackingMovesQueue — an orphan action floating in the gap. It feeds the queue, so it should live in the queue's header action slot (the SectionCard action pattern). Removes the orphan line and puts the control on the object it modifies. Artifact: ux-review-admin-cockpit.html. [Wave 3: shipped]

items.title: Client shell — finish the two-speed nav migration; retire the 12-tab legacy nav
items.notes: Behind client-ia-v2 the nav collapses from ~12 top-level tabs (Overview, Performance, Health, Strategy, Inbox, Content Plan, Plans, ROI, Brand, Results, Deep Dive, Settings) to four. Twelve is far too many for a client; while the flag is mid-rollout the product has two front doors depending on flag state. Fix: commit to the two-speed shell and retire legacy nav — Overview = verdict; Deep Dive holds Performance/Health/Rankings/Content sub-views; Inbox = loop; Settings absorbs Plans/Brand. Highest-impact move on this screen. Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 5: shipped behind client-ia-v2 flag; default-ON flip deferred to owner rollout]

items.title: Client shell — define each tab panel once; collapse duplicate mounts
items.notes: Health, Performance, Strategy, Content Plan, Brand, and Plans each render both as a standalone tab AND as a slot inside Deep Dive/Settings, with the full prop list duplicated inline (HealthTab + actionPlanSlot repeated verbatim). Two routes reach identical content and can silently drift. Fix: define each panel once (a const or a registry keyed by tab id) and reference it wherever it appears; under the two-speed shell give each surface a single home (Health/Performance/Strategy live inside Deep Dive, not also top-level). Depends on design-cd-finish-ia-v2-nav. Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 5: shipped behind client-ia-v2 flag; default-ON flip deferred to owner rollout]

items.title: Client shell — one prioritized notice region above tabs
items.notes: Above tab content sit up to three stacked notices: trial-countdown (or trial-ended) banner, a section-errors InlineBanner, and a per-tab SeoEducationTip — the same 'wall before the work' as CP-F3. Fix: one notice region with priority order (errors > trial > education tip) showing at most one at a time / docked compactly; the education tip yields to a real error. Reuse the CP-F3 consolidation pattern. Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 5: shipped behind client-ia-v2 flag; default-ON flip deferred to owner rollout]

items.title: Client shell — stop echoing the nav label as the per-tab page title
items.notes: Every tab renders a PageHeader whose title is just the active tab's label (click Health -> page says Health); the nav already shows location, so the H1 echoes it without adding info — only the subtitle carries something new. Fix: drop the redundant title echo (let subtitle + content carry the page) or make the header earn its space (verdict line, freshness stamp, the tab's primary action). Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 5: shipped behind client-ia-v2 flag; default-ON flip deferred to owner rollout]

items.title: Client shell — route trial banners through InlineBanner / warning tokens
items.notes: Trial-countdown and trial-ended banners use raw bg-amber-500/8 border-amber-500/20 and bg-red-500/8 border-red-500/20 mixed with text-accent-warning/danger token classes — a half-tokenized warning surface and a third spelling of 'amber notice' alongside InlineBanner and the cockpit staleness nudge (AC-F3). Fix: route both through InlineBanner (or its token-backed warning/danger variants) so every alert surface shares one definition. Folds into the tokenize-hardcoded-hex theme. Artifact: ux-review-client-shell.html. [Wave 0b: shipped]

items.title: Cross-cutting — promote the tinted-gradient card to a StatCard/card tone prop
items.notes: The 'tinted gradient + matching border' card treatment is hand-rolled with slightly different opacities per surface: the verdict shell uses from-teal-500/8 border-teal-500/15; the outcome StatCards pass className='bg-gradient-to-br from-emerald-500/10 border-emerald-500/20'. Fix: promote it to a first-class StatCard/card tone prop (tone='emerald' -> gradient + border baked in at one canonical opacity) so the verdict hero and outcome band share one definition and future surfaces inherit it. Artifact: ux-review-client-issue.html. [Closeout: shipped]

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — ✨ Admin Surfaces — Polish Sweeps (minors, batched per surface)
sprints.rationale: Terminal item history split from sprint-admin-surfaces-polish-2026-06-11. All 🟡 minors from the audit, one batched PR per surface. The report's per-surface minors lists are the line-item spec — items here are deliberately coarse to keep the roadmap readable.

items.title: Strategy polish sweep (report §1 minors)
items.notes: Incl. orange→teal active states, sky-* removal, aria-expanded on collapsibles, in-page nav for 16 stacked sections, dead discovery code delete, Guide claim fix. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · medium conf]: Re-scope and keep, P3. Reframe from the stale KeywordStrategy.tsx line refs to: (1) sweep sky-*/orange-* hue violations to blue/teal repo-wide (or land a pr-check hue rule), (2) delete the dead discovery block in keyword-strategy-seo-data.ts and its stale flag comments, (3) re-audit the redesigned KeywordStrategyPanel for aria-expanded + in-page nav, since the original section-stacking finding may no longer apply. The dead-code deletion is the highest-confidence, lowest-risk win. || DONE 2026-06-27: completed remaining deterministic delta. StrategySettings active state now uses teal, SEO data mode/page-limit controls expose button-group labels plus aria-pressed state, settings/business-context and StrategyDiff disclosures expose aria-expanded, strategy archetype technical styling no longer uses sky-* or text-accent-orange, and stale retired seo-generation-quality flag comments were updated to the canonical synthesis path. Re-audit found the legacy dead discovery branch already removed and the old 16-section no-nav complaint superseded by the redesigned tabbed strategy shell. Added source/unit contracts for these seams.

items.title: Local SEO polish sweep (report §3 minors)
items.notes: Incl. primary-market alphabetical fallback, degradedCount surfacing, useSetPrimaryMarket invalidation, hardcoded unit price, unsaved-changes guard, window/label copy. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · medium conf]: Keep as still-relevant but low priority (P3). At least three of the six named minors are concretely still open (useSetPrimaryMarket invalidation is a genuine data-staleness bug worth fixing; hardcoded price and degradedCount surfacing are small UX cleanups). The useSetPrimaryMarket missing invalidation is the only one with real user-visible impact and could be pulled out and fixed independently in any adjacent local-seo touch. Recommend re-verifying each of the six bullets against current code at pickup time (some may have been incidentally fixed in w6 waves), then either batching the survivors into one small sweep or opportunistically fixing the invalidation bug now. || DONE 2026-06-27: completed remaining delta. Primary-market successor promotion now uses deterministic case-insensitive label ordering instead of insertion order, the DataForSEO per-keyword cost estimate is centralized in shared local-SEO types and consumed by the setup drawer, provider-degraded refresh counts are now surfaced in the Local SEO stat grid, and contract coverage pins useSetPrimaryMarket invalidation across local SEO, Keyword Hub/KCC, Strategy, and intelligence reads. Re-audit found the error-state branch, unsaved-draft guard, and explicit refresh-budget copy already present on staging.

items.title: Schema polish sweep (report §4 minors)
items.notes: Incl. setup-hero flash, Workflow Guide dead ends, header action row layout, sub-tab bar → TabBar, page-type triplication, vocabulary/token drift, dead context fields. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P3. Framing is correct. Highest-value sub-items: adopt TabBar for the sub-tab bar (fixes a11y + ?tab= deep-link contract in one move) and consolidate the triplicated page-type option lists into the single SCHEMA_PAGE_TYPE_OPTIONS source (kills a known drift hazard and aligns with the schema-context intelligence-slice rules). Delete the dead SchemaContext fields in the same PR. || PARTIAL 2026-06-27: converted SchemaSuggester to PageHeader + TabBar with ?tab=guide receiver support, consolidated SchemaPageCard/initial picker/site-plan role controls onto the shared schema page-type option source, and removed dead SchemaContext prompt-only fields (businessContext/searchIntent/knowledgeBase/_personasBlock) from schema generation while keeping schema-plan businessContext behavior intact. Remaining delta: re-audit setup-hero flash, Workflow Guide dead ends, and broader vocabulary/token drift with screenshots. || DONE 2026-06-27: completed remaining deterministic guide/vocabulary delta. SchemaWorkflowGuide now names the live Generate Site Plan control, removes the nonexistent Save to Snapshot and Competitors-tab instructions, points prioritization to Page Intelligence/completeness summaries instead of phantom coverage/priority dashboards, and updates Schema Impact wording to match the current panel. The generator now shows a saved-results hydration state instead of flashing the setup hero before snapshots load. Added source-contract coverage to prevent the stale dead-end copy and hydration regression from returning. Remaining visual screenshot QA for header/action wrapping and broad token drift is preserved as `simp-delta-0627-schema-visual-token-recheck` rather than swallowed by this source-only closeout.

items.title: Content polish sweep (report §5 minors)
items.notes: Incl. StatusBadge registry adoption (4 hand-rolled maps), cyan→teal CTAs, dual Send-to-Client label split, briefs IA inversion, FAB collision, checkbox/hover a11y, undo on delete. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant (partially-done), P3. Re-scope: drop the briefs-IA-inversion line (fixed) and keep the rest. Highest-value sub-items are the cyan→teal CTA + dual-label split (both shippable together — I have flagged this as a spinoff task) and the StatusBadge registry adoption (kills live label drift). a11y, FAB, and WorkflowStepper items are lower priority. Bundle as a single small sweep PR. || [2026-06-24 SPINOFF DONE]: Content-briefs cyan→teal CTAs (RequestList Send/Resubmit/Send-post + BriefDetail send) + dual Send-to-Client label split (RequestList 'Send to client' status-bump vs BriefDetail 'Send brief to client' create-request) + Admin Send Convention optional inline note field on BOTH brief send paths (status-bump threads internalNote via existing PATCH; create-request path now accepts note → clientNote/internalNote, mirroring sendPostToClientForReview). || DONE 2026-06-27: completed remaining delta. RequestList now uses the shared StatusBadge registry via a content-admin domain and removes cyan review-state drift, brief row quick actions are focus-within visible instead of hover-only, the Content Pipeline guide FAB is moved above bottom-right overlays, and content brief/request deletion is delayed with a visible Undo window before the server hard-delete commits. Added contract coverage for all four cleanup seams.

items.title: Admin shell polish sweep (report §6 minors)
items.notes: Incl. tab naming drift, SchemaSuggester PageHeader/TabBar adoption, WorkspaceHome hand-rolled stat card, ad-hoc z-index scale, palette no-workspace no-ops, ?tab= cleanup comment fix, duplicate sidebar icons. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P3. Re-scope: drop the '?tab= cleanup comment fix' line (resolved) and keep the rest. Quick wins: de-duplicate the 4 sidebar icon collisions in navRegistry.tsx (pure icon swaps), convert WorkspaceHome's two hand-rolled cards to StatCard, and fix the CommandPalette no-workspace guard (hide/disable workspace-scoped items + don't addRecent on no-op). The z-index token consolidation (add --z-overlay/--z-system tiers + migrate 7 hatched raw values) is a slightly larger but worthwhile platform-foundation cleanup. || DONE 2026-06-27: completed the deterministic shell cleanup subset. De-duplicated nav registry icon collisions without changing labels/routes/gating, made CommandPalette hide workspace-scoped nav entries when no workspace is selected so they cannot become no-op recents, moved WorkspaceHome Site Health metric rendering onto StatCard via a small trailing slot for the existing MetricRing, and replaced the audited raw overlay z-index hatch values with named tokens mirrored in src/public token files and documented in BRAND_DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md. Added contract/primitive coverage for nav icon uniqueness, palette availability, WorkspaceHome primitive usage, z-index token migration, and StatCard trailing content. Deferred recommendation: SchemaSuggester PageHeader/TabBar adoption remains a separate visual polish pass because its current schemaSubTab shell is entangled with guide/generator rendering and should be verified with screenshots rather than bundled into this behavior-preserving shell sweep.

items.title: SchemaSuggester PageHeader/TabBar visual polish pass
items.notes: Follow-up from the admin shell polish sweep. Convert SchemaSuggester's local schemaSubTab/header shell to the canonical PageHeader + TabBar pattern without changing scan/generator/guide/publish behavior. Because this is visual shell work around a complex page with extracted generation/CMS/publishing workflows, do it as a focused UI PR with screenshots at desktop and narrow widths, plus existing SchemaSuggester workflow tests. Preserve literal tab ordering, guide/generator behavior, fixContext auto-generation, and bulk publish controls. || DONE 2026-06-27: SchemaSuggester now uses shared PageHeader + TabBar, supports direct ?tab=guide links, keeps generator/guide ordering and workflow behavior, and has component/source-contract coverage for the shell boundary. This PR also completed the adjacent page-type option consolidation and dead SchemaContext field cleanup from awp-schema-sweep.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — SEO Decision Engine — close the data→decision gap (8-phase program)
sprints.rationale: From the 2026-06-23 15-agent SEO audit (overall C, 'A-grade architecture wired to a C-grade decision surface'). Group A flips loops already built; Group B fixes geo correctness + adds a cost-governed foundation; Group C adds paid capabilities. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-23-seo-decision-engine-design.md. Deferred items (status: deferred) are out-of-program backlog captured so nothing is lost.

items.title: P1 — Flip keyword-value-scoring on + delete crude Hub-sort path (one PR). Keep computeOpportunityScore as gate-failure/backfill fallback.
items.notes: Done (2026-06-24). Retired the keyword-value-scoring flag; value-first scoring is unconditional. Crude Hub-sort accessors deleted; computeOpportunityScore kept as the gate-failure/backfill/projection fallback. 7 test files updated; pr-check anti-reintroduction guard added + deprecation registry advanced (hidden→removed, keyword-universe-full added as the hidden exemplar). PR to staging; staging eyeball of admin Hub + client Strategy ordering before main.

items.title: P2 — Effort priors: thread getEffortPriorDays (measured time-to-implement) into the 16 OV scorer calls, overriding DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS. Calibration-basis swap deferred to GA4 revenue grounding.
items.notes: Done (2026-06-24). Effort-priors only: getEffortPriorDays threaded per-actionType into all 16 computeOpportunityValue inputs via an effortDaysFor(type, source) helper. Inert until >=3 measured outcomes of an action type accrue (then falls back to DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS, byte-identical). The realized/predicted calibration-BASIS swap was DEFERRED (owner decision 2026-06-24): ov-calibration.ts deliberately gates the basis flip on GA4 estimatedRevenue (attributed_value is a CPC proxy today), and this program also deferred GA4 grounding. Basis flip now rides with seo-engine-deferred-revenue-grounding.

items.title: P3 — Surface AI-Overview/SERP-feature triggers from ranked_keywords serp_item_types into SerpFeatures type + content-brief AEO directive + scoring
items.notes: Done (2026-06-24). ai_overview recognized in hasSerpOpportunity (string label from ranked_keywords serp_item_types), pushed into stored serpFeatures at both enrichment build sites, added to the SerpFeatures intelligence type + seo-context aggregation + AI-context formatter + countSerpOpportunities (client serpOpportunities), a content-brief AI-Overview AEO directive, and the ContentGapRow chip maps (descriptive/plain/emoji) + ordered set. Free signal = 'this keyword triggers an AI Overview', NOT 'are we cited' (the paid P6 national-SERP fetch). Zero new credits. Brief directive is prompt-only by design (shapes the visible AI-generated brief).

items.title: P4 — Geo correctness: thread location/language through domain endpoints + workspace target-geo field (migration) + resolveWorkspaceTargetGeo()
items.notes: DONE 2026-06-24. Flag `geo-targeting` (OFF = byte-identical, incl. legacy un-versioned domain cache key via domainGeoToken — no re-warm). Interface + all 6 domain methods thread locationCode/languageCode; migration 152 target_geo column + TargetGeo type + workspaces.ts lockstep + PATCH boundary + toAdminWorkspaceView serializer + resolveWorkspaceTargetGeo() (field→local primary market→US/en). 12 caller sites threaded via workspaceProviderGeo() gate (competitor calls use CLIENT geo; backlinks excluded; getKeywordsForKeywords has no live caller yet). Admin BusinessFootprint TargetGeoEditor (curated DataForSEO country list = 2000+ISO numeric) gated behind flag. Tests: provider body/cache-key (4), PATCH boundary (3), row-mapper completeness, TargetGeoEditor render (4). Fixes silent US/English corruption for non-US clients (D-grade national/intl dimension).

items.title: P5 — Reliability + cost: retry/backoff, activate dead markCapabilityDisabled, credit-usage read path + assertCreditBudget per-tier gate
items.notes: DONE 2026-06-24. (1) Bounded retry-with-backoff in external-fetch.ts for transient 429/500/502/503/504 + network (default 2 retries on fetchProviderJson; 402/401/4xx/timeout never retried) — transient errors no longer collapse to []/null. (2) Activated the dead markCapabilityDisabled: a 40204 on backlinks now trips a 6h in-memory breaker + removed the `capability !== 'backlinks'` guard so getBacklinksProvider short-circuits to null (callers degrade). (3) NEW server/credit-budget-gate.ts: CREDIT_BUDGETS {free:0, growth:2000, premium:Infinity} + evaluateCreditBudget + assertCreditBudget cross-phase contract (P6-P8 call at route entry). Owner decision: OBSERVE-ONLY launch (budgetEnforcementEnabled=false; assertCreditBudget logs would-block, never throws; flip one let to hard-enforce). (4) Health card quotaStatus now reflects MTD budget vs usage; (5) AIUsageSection surfaces the served-but-dark dataforseo/dataforseoDaily (credits/calls/cache-hit-rate + daily chart). DEVIATIONS from audit: U4 (provider-internal gate call) reverted — created a heavy provider→gate→workspaces import cycle that broke provider tests; gating belongs at P6-P8 route entry per the audit's own contract note. U7 (NotificationBell budget-blocked) deferred — observe-only means no blocks happen yet; CreditBudgetError contract is ready for the enforcement flip. Tests: retry (7), budget-gate (12), capability-breaker (8); updated seo-provider-routing + local-visibility for the new behaviors. No feature flag (reliability/cost infra).

items.title: P6 — National advanced-SERP rank + AI-Overview citation layer: new job, true rank + features, serp_feature_opportunity insight, historical backfill
items.notes: DONE 2026-06-24. Flag national-serp-tracking (SEO Decision Engine group; OFF=byte-identical). Built fixture-grounded U0-U8. (U0) migration 153 serp_snapshots — true SERP rank + features time series (position/matched_url NULLable; parallel to GSC rank_snapshots, joined on (ws,date,query), never conflated); KeywordCommandCenterMetrics +nationalPosition/matchedUrl/serpFeatures/aiOverviewCited/aiOverviewPresent (ADDITIVE — never overrides GSC currentPosition, so value-score/skinny-replay invariant holds); serp_feature_opportunity 7-part insight lockstep; NationalSerpProviderRequest/Result + optional getNationalSerp. (U1) parseNationalSerp pure+fixture-validated (rank_group among type==='organic' only for client rank; aggregated top-level references[].domain for AI-Overview citation; www-strip domain match) + getNationalSerp reusing runDataForSeoOperation/cache168h/credit-logging. (U2) serp-snapshots-store (tri-state mapper, parseJsonSafeArray, transaction upsert, keywordComparisonKey-normalized query). (U3) runNationalSerpRefreshJob ported from runLocalSeoRefreshJob (concurrency=1 global+per-ws hasActiveJob, memory headroom, cancel checks, INCREMENTAL flush so cancel keeps paid reads) + POST /api/rank-tracking/:ws/refresh-national gated flag→Growth+ tier→observe-only assertCreditBudget→serialization; 100-kw cap; target-geo (P4) threaded. (U4) computeSerpFeatureOpportunities fires ONLY on the ownership-guarded AI-Overview-uncited signal (featured-snippet trigger dropped after review — parser has presence not ownership; capture deferred to P7); deleteStaleInsightsByType unconditional (bridge rule). (U2b) admin metrics overlay in keyword-command-center. (client) serp_feature_opportunity narrative projector ('Google's AI Overview is answering X without citing you'). (U6) KeywordDetailDrawer live-SERP rank + ranking URL + serpBadges (extracted to shared/serpFeatureBadges) + AI-Overview citation badge emerald(cited)/zinc(not), never purple; flag-gated 'Refresh national ranks' Hub button. WS_EVENTS.SERP_SNAPSHOTS_REFRESHED (server broadcast + centralized useWsInvalidation + KeywordHub handler + platform-domain-event + background-job-matrix registrations). Client metrics admin-only (no public-portal serializer); client sees only the insight narrative. DEVIATIONS from audit: nationalPosition is a SEPARATE field (not a currentPosition override) to preserve the value-score invariant; 'public serialization of metrics' was N/A (no public command-center endpoint) — the client surface is the insight narrative instead; historical backfill stays deferred (owner decision). Scaled multi-agent review (4 reviewers): security + compliance clean; fixed 2 Important (snippet false-positive, cancel-discards-batch) + 2 Minor. Tests: parser(5)+store(6)+narrative(2)+drawer(2)+route integration(4); full unit 14129 / component 4123 / contract 1401 green.

items.title: P7 — GBP + reviews local layer: business_listings_search + capture ratings → review-gap recs
items.notes: DONE 2026-06-24. Flag local-gbp (SEO Decision Engine group; PAID surface OFF=byte-identical). FREE+PAID (audit-validated split). FREE half (unflagged, P3 precedent): parseListingRating extracts rating.value/votes_count from local_pack items the local refresh already fetches — populates LocalVisibilityBusinessResult.rating/reviewCount ($0). PAID half: getBusinessListings (business_data/business_listings_search, reuses runDataForSeoOperation/cache24h/credit-breaker) — category search (competitors, sorted by votes_count) + TITLE search (the owner, who's often too review-poor to surface in a count-sorted search); migration 154 business_listing_snapshots (own + competitors via is_owned; rating/review_count NULLable, zero reviews = absent never 0); business-listings-store. local-gbp-refresh job (ported from national-serp: serialization, memory, cancel, incremental flush; owner assigned by parser-VERIFIED isOwned, not result[0]) + POST /api/local-seo/:ws/refresh-gbp gated flag→Growth+→observe-only budget→serialization. DELIVERABLE: review-gap + GBP-completeness RECOMMENDATIONS (reuse local_visibility RecType, no new insight) — review-gap (count≥10 OR star≥0.3 vs top competitor with real review count) + GBP-completeness (profile richness <60/100; claim-status intentionally NOT a trigger — is_claimed defaults true so unclaimed owners aren't returned). Admin GbpReviewsPanel (reviews-vs-competitors blue, completeness scoreColorClass, aggregates only, never purple) + GET /api/local-seo/:ws/gbp-reviews + LocalSeoSlice.reviewSummary (AI context). WS LOCAL_GBP_SNAPSHOTS_REFRESHED full registry lockstep. DEVIATIONS: getBusinessListings gained optional title (owner lookup); shared deriveGbpCompletenessScore (parser + recs); 'claimed' reframed to profile-completeness after scaled review found it never reliably false. Scaled 4-agent review: security + PII clean (no review text/authors/contact_info captured); fixed 3 Important (dead unclaimed branches, owner name-collision, missing panel invalidation key) + 2 Minor. Tests: listing-rating(5)+parser(6)+store(9)+recs(2)+panel(3)+slice+route integration(4); full unit 14151 / component 4126 / contract green.

items.title: P8 — AI-visibility slice: ai_optimization/llm_mentions_* → 'are we cited by LLMs' KPI + AEO before/after proof
items.notes: DONE 2026-06-24 — FINAL phase of the SEO Decision Engine program. Flag ai-visibility (SEO Decision Engine group; OFF=byte-identical). Mentions-DB only (no direct prompting); KPI-only deliverable (no new rec/insight — the existing aeo-* recs are the actions). getLlmMentions provider (ai_optimization/llm_mentions/aggregated_metrics, reuses runDataForSeoOperation/cache336h/40204-breaker; VERIFY-ON-LIVE note on the exact REST envelope — response shape is fixture-validated) + parseLlmMentions. Migration 155 llm_mention_snapshots (time-series; mentions/ai_search_volume NULLable → absent=0 never invented). HEADLINE = share-of-voice (client BRAND ÷ all co-mentioned brand_entities_title, like-to-like) — distinct from mention VOLUME (domain citations). llm-mentions-refresh job (ported national-serp pattern; resolves ownerBrandNames from workspace.name + domain-root fallback) + POST /api/rank-tracking/:ws/refresh-ai-visibility + GET .../ai-visibility, gated flag→Growth+→observe-only budget→serialization. Admin AiVisibilityPanel (share-of-voice MetricRing, mention volume, mentions-over-time trend sparkline = before/after proof, competitor share-of-voice, source-domain AEO targets; blue data/score colors, never purple) mounted in KeywordHub. SeoContextSlice.aiVisibility → AdminChat/AI context. WS LLM_MENTIONS_SNAPSHOTS_REFRESHED full registry lockstep. DEVIATIONS: route under /api/rank-tracking (existing registered group, not /api/strategy); CLIENT-facing KPI deferred (see seo-engine-followon-ai-visibility-client-kpi) — it needs accrued snapshots to be a meaningful before/after. Scaled 4-agent review: security + PII + compliance clean (no LLM transcripts captured — aggregates only); fixed 3 Important (all share-of-voice: 'not measured' undefined vs misleading 0%, over-matching brand matcher, blank-name degradation). Tests: parser(13)+store(7)+panel(4)+route integration(5); full unit 14170 / component 4129 / contract 1401 green. SEO Decision Engine P1-P8 COMPLETE.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — The Issue (Client) — Verdict-First Trust Spine
sprints.rationale: Invert the client The Issue dashboard from a 0-100 vanity-ring/content-plan hero into a verdict-first trust spine that leads with a dollarized, baseline-anchored, GA4-grounded outcome verdict. P0 = the trust spine on GA4 estimates (verdict + outcome count + money frame, baseline-anchored to workspace.createdAt, eventConfig-pinned), shipped behind the-issue-client-spine master flag (OFF = byte-identical). P1 = reconciliation/return-hook/segment-inserts/next-bets, declared OFF and deferred until P0 is merged + green on staging.

items.title: The Issue (Client) P1 — return hook (forwardable one-pager export [P1b] + event-driven EMAIL pull-back [P1c]; NO SMS)
items.notes: Flag: the-issue-client-return-hook (declared OFF, staging-validation rollout to watch delivery cost first). [P1b BUILT + review-fixed 2026-06-20] docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-the-issue-client-p1b-plan.md — admin setup-readiness checklist (IssueSetupReadiness) + admin/client named-leads + forwardable one-pager export (print-from-browser, NO PDF lib); D7 PII boundary held; 12-finding scaled review all fixed (1 Important + 7 Minor). See FEATURE_AUDIT #531. [P1c BUILT + PR-READY 2026-06-20] docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-the-issue-client-p1c-plan.md — EMAIL-ONLY (no SMS, owner-locked) weekly event-gated return-hook: a new weekly cron (server/return-hook-cron.ts, clone of strategy-issue-cron) sends ONE consolidated client_return_hook digest (new customers/leads + new measured money + decision-still-waiting) ONLY when ≥1 section has content, through the EXISTING email stack (nodemailer queue + throttle-exempt 'return' category — the ISO-week marker, stamped only on a confirmed enqueue, is the authoritative cap — + workspace_primary recipient policy). Additive — no new transport built (7-area infra discovery confirmed it exists). Owner decisions: weekly digest only-when-something-happened; match-existing opt-out + 'reply to stop' footer (platform-wide preference center deferred → email-preference-center). Migration 151 (last_return_hook_sent_week_of weekly marker). Money section activity-gated (only on new-leads-this-week + measured_action verdict). 19 new tests; full suite 26806 green; flag-OFF byte-identical. See FEATURE_AUDIT #532. Status stays pending until ALL of P1 is soaked on staging + the legacy-cutover/teardown (owner-locked sequence: build all P1 → one soak + cutover at the end). || [DELTA AUDIT 2026-06-24 · already-done]: both halves built + flag-gated — P1b one-pager export + P1c email pull-back via return-hook-cron.ts (idempotency mig 151). Residual is staging-soak + cutover gate, not unbuilt code.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — Strategy Page — Decision-First Redesign
sprints.rationale: Up-level the admin seo-strategy page from a ~30-section passive scroll into a decision-first 3-band IA (Decide/Act/Reference). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-16-strategy-page-decision-first-redesign-design.md.

items.title: Phase 0 — decompose KeywordStrategy.tsx into hooks + leaves

items.title: Phase 5a — strategy_history snapshot on manual PATCH (What Changed misattribution fix)
items.notes: Done 2026-06-17 (PR #1262, merged to staging). First of three Phase 5 sub-PRs. The manual PATCH strategy-edit path (routes/keyword-strategy.ts) did NOT snapshot strategy_history (only the AI-regen writeKeywordStrategy did), so StrategyDiff ('What Changed') misattributed human edits to the last AI regeneration. Extracted the snapshot INSERT+prune-to-5 from keyword-strategy-persistence.ts into a reusable snapshotStrategyHistory(workspaceId, previousStrategy, priorArrays) helper (guards on prior generatedAt; must run inside db.transaction BEFORE the replaceAll/upsert clobber). Called it at the top of the PATCH applyPatch transaction (reads prior table-backed arrays + uses the PRIOR generatedAt). Behavior-preserving refactor of the regen path. Integration test: PATCH-then-/diff attributes the edit to the new boundary; no-blob PATCH records no snapshot (guard). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-strategy-redesign-phase-5-signal-generation.md (decisions: cron conservative+kill-switch behind signal-auto-recompute flag; on-mutation always-enqueue).

Threaded computedAt (MAX insight computed_at) onto the /signals payload (all 3 return branches) + SignalsResponse. IntelligenceSignals card: 'Computed X ago' muted caption (timeAgo) + teal 'Recompute now' Button (variant=link) in the SectionCard action slot, wired via useRecomputeSignals (POST → trackJob; NotificationBell surfaces progress; the existing INTELLIGENCE_SIGNALS_UPDATED broadcast auto-invalidates — no 2nd broadcast). Worker FM-2 + component tests. LESSON: adding to BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES requires a lockstep entry in tests/helpers/background-job-test-matrix.ts (BACKGROUND_JOB_LIFECYCLE_MATRIX) or background-job-coverage-contract fails. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-strategy-redesign-phase-5-signal-generation.md.

items.title: Cannibalization triage — operator-selectable keeper (override the auto-derived canonical page)
items.notes: Phase 3 follow-on. Today CannibalizationTriage auto-derives the keeper page (item.canonicalPath if set, else best-ranking via keeperPathOf). Owner wants to OVERRIDE which competing page is the keeper (real example: keep a keyword on a services page, not the auto-picked insights page). Scope: a per-issue keeper picker; the chosen keeper must flow into (a) which pages show 'Fix in editor' (non-keepers = duplicates), and (b) the Send-to-client payload canonicalPath. KEY CONSTRAINT: the override MUST persist across strategy regeneration — cannibalization_issues is delete-then-reinsert (replaceAllCannibalizationIssues clobbers canonical_path), so the operator's keeper choice can't live on that row. Store it durably keyed by normalized keyword (mirror the 3b-i resolution pattern: a tracked record / small override table / preserved-metadata Map on the delete-reinsert), and have keeperPathOf prefer the operator override over the auto-derivation. Do after the current Strategy redesign batch. Owner expects further refinements to surface as Strategy gets exercised. || [DELTA AUDIT 2026-06-24 · already-done]: fully built — mig 141 cannibalization_keeper_override keyed on order-independent url_set_key (clobber-immune), applied at the read boundary (cannibalization-issues.ts:176), UI KeeperSelector.tsx. The durable-key design satisfies the regen-persistence constraint.

items.title: Strategy redesign Phase 4 — fold Intelligence Signals into the cockpit as real recs
items.notes: Done 2026-06-19 (PR #1286, P4 Wave 1 Lane A). Gated behind the strategy-signal-fold child flag (default OFF). Mints Intelligence Signals as real recs at strategy-gen time (mintSignalRecs inside generateRecommendations, AFTER carry-over) so the existing RECOMMENDATIONS_UPDATED broadcast + applyLifecycleCarryOver cover folded signals automatically; dedup via signal:<insightId> merge key. CRITICAL fix (caught in review): signal-sourced recs are exempted from the auto-resolve sweep — their producer never re-emits them, so 'absent from newSources' is always a false positive; without the exemption every un-actioned folded signal flipped to '✓ Auto-resolved' on the next daily/on-mutation recompute. Guarded by a status-continuity regression test (server/__tests__/signal-fold-carry-over.test.ts). Carry-over confirmed O(n). flag-OFF keeps the standalone IntelligenceSignals card (deletion deferred to the strategy-command-center cutover). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-18-strategy-redesign-synthesized-plan.md.
