sprints.name: 🗃️ TERMINAL HISTORY — 🎯 Keyword Value — propagation, money, GSC signals, client display
sprints.rationale: Terminal item history split from sprint-keyword-value-amplification-2026-06-05. Phase 0–2 of keyword-value-scoring shipped the value-first score into the Hub sort + content-gap spine, but a wiring audit found the new score is still siloed: it does NOT propagate to briefs / content-gen / titles-metas / schema / internal-linking / content-plan / AI-context, and the client briefing actively demotes it (sorts by raw volume). This sprint propagates ONE consistent value score everywhere, turns it into dollars, surfaces the per-keyword GSC data already collected-but-discarded (striking-distance, momentum), and builds the client-facing layer — all additive, reusing signals already in hand, no new data collection.

items.title: Wire resolved value score into content-plan, briefs, and client-briefing ranking
items.notes: Wave 1. Four surfaces rank the SAME keywords four contradictory ways: Hub uses valueScore, content-plan uses legacy opportunityScore (keyword-recommendations.ts:159), the client briefing sorts by raw VOLUME buckets (briefing-client-projection.ts:81, demoting high-value low-volume gaps), titles/metas lead by strategy slot order. Make them all rank by the single Hub valueScore. HIGHEST-PRIORITY — fixes the client briefing sort, the exact surface the client keyword display sits on. Flag-gated where it changes ranking. || PLACEMENT [KEYSTONE — architecture decision, brainstorm first]: reconcile the new keyword-value-score vs the existing opportunity-value OV model (two value models today — Hub valueScore is a bare number; OV exposes components + is what client surfaces render). Decide ONE source of truth, then unify ranking across content-plan (keyword-recommendations.ts:159), titles/metas, briefs, client-briefing (fix the volume-bucket sort briefing-client-projection.ts:81), internal-linking. ALSO absorbs the only real need of the DROPPED kwv-client-wins-this-month: re-rank named rank-movement wins on InsightsBriefingPage so the most valuable named mover leads. || KEYSTONE FOUNDATION DONE (PR 1 + PR 2, 2026-06-05): one classifier (deriveValueIntent — shared by both layers; comparison-intent drift closed), layered contract documented (Layer 1 = computeKeywordValueScore / Layer 2 = computeOpportunityValue; surface ownership rule), and KeywordValueComponents interface exposed via computeKeywordValueComponents (commercialValue/demand/winnability/localMultiplier/intent). Signal-gate parity locked by test. 4 scalar callers byte-identical. Surface re-ranking (content-plan/briefs/client-briefing) remains this item's own work. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: KEEP — still-relevant, correctly framed. The keystone architecture decision (one source of truth: Layer 1 valueScore vs Layer 2 OV with surface-ownership rule) is already made and documented, so this is now pure wiring work. Highest-value sub-task remains fixing the client-briefing volume-bucket sort (briefing-client-projection.ts:69-83) to rank by the resolved value score — it is the exact surface the client sees. Re-scope the title to 'Wire the resolved value score into content-plan/briefs/client-briefing ranking' since the foundation is no longer pending. || DONE 2026-06-27: completed the re-scoped wiring delta. Extracted the shared workspace scoring-context builder to server/scoring/keyword-value-context.ts while preserving the KCC buildValueScoringConfig wrapper; content-plan keyword recommendations now use computeKeywordValueScore as the deterministic base before existing strategic-fit/outcome/cannibalization/authority adjustments; client briefing content-gap recommendations now sort by a private Layer-1 value sort score when real ranking signals exist while preserving the public opportunityScore display field and legacy score-only fallback. Titles/metas/schema and internal-linking remain tracked by their dedicated Wave 5 items.

items.title: Named 28-day momentum + auto rank-gain/rank-loss recommendations
items.notes: Wave 3. Named risers/fallers ('emergency dentist #19→#8 +340 impr; [service] near me #4→#9 — defend it') from getLatestRanks change + getRankHistory 90-day slope. AND mint defend/push recommendations from tracked movement — the rec surface is 100% blind to rank movement today (ranking_mover insight exists but is never converted to a Recommendation). Reuses kwv-gsc-per-keyword-context. || PLACEMENT [split]: named risers/fallers ALREADY ship — ranking_mover insight + ranking-mover.ts client win story (signal-story-registry.ts:410 -> InsightsBriefingPage). The genuinely-NEW half = defend/push RECOMMENDATIONS: add a rank/defend/push RecType + RecActionType + RecSourceCategory via the four-line contract in recommendations.ts (overlaps the existing strategy:ranking-opportunity source). Do NOT re-render named movers as a new surface. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: KEEP — still-relevant, but re-scope to ONLY the movement-driven defend/push recommendation half (do not re-render named movers; they already ship). The four-line RecType/RecActionType/RecSourceCategory contract in recommendations.ts is the insertion point; reuse the existing strategyRankingOpp source family but feed it rank-history slope (riser→push, faller→defend) rather than the static currentPosition snapshot. The static 4-20 push rec partially covers 'push' but adds no 'defend' and ignores momentum. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OBSOLETE-CLOSE: named movement, rank-drop detection, work-queue surfacing, and event-driven recommendation re-ranking ship. Separate defend/push auto-minting would duplicate those paths without evidence of an action gap.

items.title: Titles/metas/schema: lead with the highest-value keyword + revive the dead schema searchIntent field
items.notes: Wave 5 (wiring-audit gap). Title/meta + schema generation ARE keyword-targeted per page but get NO prioritization signal — they lead by strategy slot order, not value, and SchemaContext.searchIntent is declared (schema-suggester.ts:314) but never populated or consumed (dead field). Make seo-copy + buildSchemaContext lead with the highest-value target keyword and use intent to shape schema (Article vs Service/FAQ). HOME: existing seo-copy + buildSchemaContext (server/schema/context-builder.ts). PLACEMENT [enrich existing generation path]. | Absorbs admin-audit W6.2 (2026-06-11, report §4): populate dead ctx.searchIntent + per-page value score in buildSchemaContext; sort schema page list + bulk-generation order by value. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: KEEP — still-relevant, correctly framed. Two concrete, low-risk changes: (a) make copy-generation.ts lead with the highest-value target keyword for the page (using the resolved value score from the kwv-one-score foundation), and (b) populate the dead ctx.searchIntent in buildSchemaContext (context-builder.ts) + sort the schema page list and bulk-generation order by per-page value, and use intent to shape schema type (Article vs Service/FAQ). Depends on kwv-one-score's resolved value score being callable from these generation paths. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OBSOLETE-CLOSE: page primary keyword is already authoritative, schema plans own intent, and unused SchemaContext.searchIntent was deliberately removed and contract-tested.

items.title: Re-ground client-facing estimatedGain in real traffic-at-risk + accumulated outcomes
items.notes: Audit finding Q7: estimatedGain recovery-% are static per-check constants ('could recover 15-30%'), identical for every workspace/page. Deferred from PR3/PR6 (client-facing + $-exposure). Once OV is live, derive the client-safe gain string from the page's actual traffic-at-risk + median realized attributed_value — never a raw $/wk to the client. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Re-scope and keep as a smaller follow-up. The static-constant credibility risk (the audit's actual concern) is resolved — close that part. Remaining work: once a workspace has conclusive outcome history, blend the page's median realized attributed_value (from action_outcomes via getCalibrationOutcomes / the new EMV-calibration ratio) into buildOvGainString's band selection so the client phrasing reflects what has actually been realized, not just the predicted proxy. This is naturally the same 'P6 basis flip' as ov-calibration-realized-vs-predicted-emv — bundle them. Still client-safe-non-dollarized. || [BUNDLE 2026-06-24]: ship together with ov-calibration-realized-vs-predicted-emv — same P6 realized-vs-predicted-EMV basis flip. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] RESCOPE-MERGE into kwv-conversion-grounded-vpc so real attribution and client gain bands do not maintain a second calibration path.

items.title: Upgrade OV calibration from win-rate proxy to realized-vs-predicted EMV
items.notes: PR5 ships a documented win-rate-derived calibration because predicted EMV isn't persisted to divide against. Persist predicted emvPerWeek at rec-generation time (or join via ov_divergence), then swap computeOvCalibration to median(realized attributed_value / predicted EMV) per (actionType, difficultyBucket). Clamp + identity-gate contract stays. Needs accumulated outcome history. || PARTIAL 2026-06-11 (A5, audit #20): the substrate now exists — predictedEmv is snapshotted on ALL completion paths (live PATCH + backfill, plus a NULL-snapshot repair pass in server/outcome-backfill.ts), and the weekly cron server/outcome-emv-calibration.ts (migration 131, outcome_emv_calibration) persists median(attributed_value/predicted_emv) per (workspace, actionType) with an honest conclusive/inconclusive gate at 5 pairs, plus median time-to-completion effort priors (getEffortPriorDays). REMAINING: flip computeOvCalibration's basis to consume getEmvCalibrationForWorkspace() (per-actionType; difficultyBucket needs difficulty persisted on actions) and thread effort priors into OpportunityInput.effortDays. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep, framed correctly, as a focused ~3-5h item — it is the highest-leverage of the OV set because the dead-ended substrate is pure waste until consumed. Two concrete hookups: flip computeOvCalibration to read getEmvCalibrationForWorkspace() (per-actionType median ratio, same clamp+identity-gate) with the win-rate proxy as the <5-pair fallback; thread getEffortPriorDays() into OpportunityInput.effortDays at the recommendations.ts push sites. Defer the difficultyBucket dimension to a separate item (needs a new difficulty column on tracked_actions first) — or drop it if per-actionType calibration proves sufficient. || [BUNDLE 2026-06-24]: ship together with ov-estimatedgain-real-attribution — same P6 basis flip. Highest-leverage of the OV set: flip computeOvCalibration to call getEmvCalibrationForWorkspace() (zero consumers today) + thread getEffortPriorDays(); defer the difficultyBucket dimension (needs a new column). || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] RESCOPE-MERGE into kwv-conversion-grounded-vpc. Measured effort priors ship; the remaining realized-value basis flip must move with GA4 value grounding.

Proceed when ready: in the competitor detector, when the overtaken keyword resolves to no covering rec, mint a defensive 'technical'/content rec with an OV opportunity attached. Validate against duplicate-minting on repeated competitor cron runs (use a buildMergeKey-style dedup). || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OBSOLETE-CLOSE: competitor alert-to-timing/re-rank and operator-triggered idempotent mint paths ship. Auto-minting adds noise under the current curation model.

sprints.name: 🗃️ TERMINAL HISTORY — Intelligence Quality Consolidation — Recommendations & AI Content
sprints.rationale: Terminal item history split from sprint-intelligence-quality-consolidation. Close the root quality gaps in platform insights and AI content generation by joining mature subsystems through the existing intelligence conventions instead of hand-rolling context, scoring, narrative, and evidence handling per feature. The target architecture is: workspace data -> intelligence slices -> shared generation context -> AI operation contract -> deterministic recommendation/story output -> tracked action/outcome -> future intelligence. The remaining keyword work extends this consolidation into a full keyword operating loop: provider source data -> shared keyword intelligence -> strategy/recommendation judgment -> rank-tracking propagation -> content/action follow-through -> outcome feedback.

Candidate signals include rating, review count, review velocity, review themes, recent negative review posture, response opportunities, and competitor review gaps. This should feed local SEO reporting/recommendations and content/trust next actions, not auto-post reviews or responses. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant; correctly framed. The local-pack v1 precondition is now satisfied. The item's guardrails (provider/cost guardrails, feed reporting/recommendations not auto-respond) remain appropriate. Recommend pairing this with intel-quality-google-business-profile-health as a single 'local reputation' build since both depend on the same business-data provider integration and would share the GBP/business-listing fetch path — sequencing them together avoids building the provider plumbing twice. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DONE: aggregate review/completeness intelligence, authenticated review read model, approval/publishing loop, and rebuilt Local Presence Reviews pipeline ship.

[Platform health recommendation pass 2026-05-26: promoted priority because client surfaces still rely on legacy local_pack signaling while LocalSeoSlice is admin-only.] || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant; correctly framed and now the most actionable of the local set (P1, admin data exists, only client-surface exposure + copy/tier gating remain). The item is correctly framed as a deliberate exposure decision pending data-quality/copy sign-off. BUT it shares a real dependency with intel-quality-keyword-per-market-relevance: exposing per-market visibility to clients before the cross-market-noise fix risks showing clients the 27.5% wrong-market 'top local results'. Recommend either (a) ship client rollout for single-market workspaces first (no cross-market noise), or (b) sequence the per-market relevance fix before multi-market client exposure. items.title: Schema engagement signals — wordCount, timeRequired, speakable, educationalLevel
items.notes: Article.wordCount shipped in the Blog/Article Schema Quality Hardening pass (May 2026) using visible content extraction instead of full articleBody duplication. Remaining quick-win Tier-4 fields with data the platform already has: Article.timeRequired = wordCount/250wpm rounded; WebPage.speakable with cssSelector pointing to .lead/.intro/first-paragraph — voice-assistant rich result; Article.educationalLevel via AI classification (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced). All wire through existing PageData typed boundary. No new admin UI required for the basics; speakable cssSelector is a per-template constant. Independent rich-result eligibility unlock for Google Article + voice-search amplification. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, re-scoped: remove wordCount from the title (it's shipped) and re-frame as the three remaining Tier-4 quick-wins. timeRequired = wordCount/250 is trivial since wordCount already exists. educationalLevel needs a small AI classification step. speakable cssSelector is a per-template constant — and is the SAME deliverable claimed by schema-page-element-catalog-pr3, so consolidate the two: make this item the single owner of speakable and close the PR3 speakable scope here. items.title: Per-workspace activity_log retention (replace global 500-row cap)
items.notes: AUDIT high/small. server/activity-log.ts:166 MAX_ENTRIES=500 is GLOBAL; pruneOldest (lines 265-269) deletes oldest across ALL workspaces. Already pinned at 500 rows for 7 workspaces → at 70 workspaces each keeps ~7 entries, silently degrading client activity feeds + engagement signals. Fix: per-workspace cap or time cutoff (created_at < now-180d); move prune to a schedule, not synchronously on every insert (231 call sites hit a COUNT(*)+prune in a hot path). || DONE 2026-06-25: replaced the global activity_log cap with scheduled per-workspace retention (`MAX_ENTRIES_PER_WORKSPACE`) run through the existing daily data-retention cycle, so high-volume workspaces cannot evict quiet workspace feeds and `addActivity()` no longer runs count/prune work in the hot insert path. Added unit coverage proving quiet-workspace rows survive unrelated noisy-workspace writes and busy workspaces prune to 500 during the retention sweep.

items.title: Normalize recommendation_sets out of its JSON blob
items.notes: AUDIT high/med. recommendation_sets is "workspace_id PRIMARY KEY ... recommendations TEXT" — one row holds the whole array (avg 222KB, max 492KB, one workspace at 210 recs). updateRecommendationStatus() (recommendations.ts:415-436) parses+rewrites the ENTIRE blob for a single status flip; loadRecommendations is on warm intelligence/chat paths. Same anti-pattern migrations 088-090 fixed for keyword_gaps/topic_clusters. Fix: dedicated recommendations table (status/source/impact indexed); status updates become single-row UPDATEs. Add a pr-check rule for new large filterable JSON-array columns. || DONE 2026-06-25: added `recommendation_items` as the row-authoritative store while keeping `recommendation_sets` for generated_at/summary and legacy fallback. `loadRecommendations()` now reads normalized rows when present, falls back to the legacy blob when absent, and row-level status/lifecycle mutations update only the target row plus summary instead of rewriting the whole blob. Extracted public recommendation projection and route mutation helpers, added `docs/rules/recommendation-storage.md`, and added a pr-check ratchet (`data/json-array-column-baseline.json`) that blocks new unreviewed `TEXT DEFAULT '[]'` migration columns.

items.title: Break intelligence-facade circular deps + add madge ratchet
items.notes: AUDIT high. 109 backend circular-dependency cycles funnel through workspace-intelligence.ts. (a) Extract invalidateIntelligenceCache (workspace-intelligence.ts:128, 49 importers) + formatPersonasForPrompt into leaf modules (intelligence/intelligence-cache.ts, intelligence/persona-format.ts); the keyword-feedback.ts:21 → helpers.ts:11 back-edge closes ~60 of 109 cycles. (b) Make slice-metadata-registry.ts slice refs lazy import thunks (it eagerly imports all 14 slices). (c) Add a madge --circular CI ratchet (madge already a devDependency, runs clean) baselined 109 server / 9 frontend, fail-on-increase; plus a routes→db/index import guard (only 6/93 route files touch the DB today). Re-export moved fns for one release; verify with madge. || DONE 2026-06-25: moved cache invalidation to `server/intelligence/cache-invalidation.ts`, shared cache state to `server/intelligence/cache-state.ts`, and persona formatting to `server/intelligence/persona-format.ts` while preserving facade re-exports. Migrated production invalidation/persona consumers off `workspace-intelligence.ts`, converted slice metadata registry assemblers to lazy dynamic imports, installed `madge`, and added `npm run check:circular-deps` plus a CI gate. Post-refactor ratchet baseline is 92 server cycles / 10 src cycles; the direct route DB import guard documents the current 7 route exceptions and blocks new ones. Added leaf invalidation and source-contract coverage.

items.title: Reconcile the 3 disagreeing position→CTR curves
items.notes: AUDIT high/med (correctness footgun). Three models disagree on "is this page underperforming?": scoring/ctr-curve.ts:46 industryCtr() (28%@pos1, canonical), analytics-intelligence.ts:131 expectedCtrForPosition() (30%@pos1, separate table), and an inline avgPos<=3?8:... (8%@pos1-3) copy-pasted into webflow-seo-bulk-rewrite-job.ts:127, routes/webflow-seo-rewrite.ts:77, routes/webflow-seo-bulk-rewrite.ts:135. Fix: make ctr-curve.ts sole owner; expectedCtrForPosition calls it; extract the triplicated Webflow block into one ctrUnderperformanceFlag() helper. || DONE 2026-06-25: `expectedCtrForPosition()` now delegates to canonical `industryCtr()`, and the Webflow rewrite route, bulk route, and background job share `ctrUnderperformanceFlag()` from `webflow-seo-rewrite-utils.ts`. Updated CTR expectations and helper coverage.

items.title: Disambiguate the two normalizePageUrl functions
items.notes: AUDIT high/small (silent page-identity mismatch). helpers.ts:161 re-exports the path-normalizer from shared/page-address-utils.ts:11; analytics-intelligence.ts:59 defines its OWN that preserves origin — and imports the shared one aliased as normalizePagePath, using both. Both feed dedup Map keys, so importing the wrong one silently produces mismatched page identities (~50 modules reference the name). Fix: rename the origin-preserving one to normalizePageUrlWithOrigin; drop the confusing local alias. || DONE 2026-06-25: renamed the analytics-only origin-preserving helper to `normalizePageUrlWithOrigin`, switched analytics path-only call sites back to the canonical `normalizePageUrl`, and updated unit coverage to make the distinction explicit.

The facade keeps the public exports and orchestrates the pipeline while prompt templates, local PageMapping/MasterStrategyData contracts, closed-set helpers, hard filters, requested re-add, and synthesis backfill live in leaf modules. Added docs/rules/keyword-strategy-synthesis.md plus source-boundary, prompt-stage, named-op, and persistence field-survival tests proving rich page-map/content-gap fields survive normalized storage while transient requested markers do not persist.

items.title: Remove GitHub Actions Node 20 deprecation warnings
items.source: Deferred CI hygiene observed during Platform Simplification 2026-06-26
items.notes: Observed during PR/staging watches: E2E annotations report actions/cache@v4, actions/download-artifact@v5, and actions/upload-artifact@v5 targeting Node.js 20 while GitHub forces them onto Node 24. Baseline on fresh branch: default pr-check reported 1011 changed files and took 171.22s; after fix on this branch: 2 changed files and 0.75s. pr-check:all remains full scan (baseline 88.10s). Added regression coverage in tests/pr-check.test.ts for local staging/main divergence, GITHUB_BASE_REF, push fallback, untracked files, deleted files, diff-slice consistency, batched pattern scans, and source-cache behavior.

items.notes: Append-only cleanup item for the current Platform Simplification sprint. Goal: reduce roadmap mental load without hiding real work. Do NOT remove or rewrite items from this current sprint except to add verified completion notes as they ship. Outside this sprint, audit small/stale sprint buckets and either (a) mark items done only when code/docs prove they are already shipped, (b) combine duplicate or superseded items into a clearer parent with provenance preserved, (c) reclassify product-judgment work as OWNER CALL / BRAINSTORM FIRST / DEFERRED with explicit re-entry criteria, or (d) archive/park stale one-off sprints only after every item is moved or closed with notes. Initial audit targets: `sprint-core-features-audit-deferred-2026-06-10` (2 parked items), `sprint-admin-surfaces-polish-2026-06-11` (5 broad polish sweeps likely suitable for triage/closeout), `sprint-platform-simplification-explorers-2026-06-09` (single remaining P2 simplification item), client-dashboard owner-call items (`tier-model-rediscussion`, `public-strategy-raw-gap-fields`, `cda-sc6-changes-requested-visibility`), and stale deferred P3/P4 items that say 'do not act', 'down the road', or require external/provider budget. Verification: JSON parse, `npx tsx scripts/sort-roadmap.ts` if item ordering changes, and PR notes listing every item closed/moved/reclassified with before/after IDs. || DONE 2026-06-27: completed a conservative roadmap-noise pass without touching protected current/new sprint work. items.title: SQLite + read-path tuning (PRAGMAs, blob reads, discovered_queries retention, slice parallelism)
items.notes: AUDIT low-med (bundle). (a) discovered_queries (largest table, 7,107 rows) has NO prune anywhere (unlike rank_snapshots/roi_snapshots which prune by date); getLostVisibilityKeys builds an unbounded in-memory Set — add a retention policy + LIMIT. (b) Default SQLite PRAGMAs leave headroom: add synchronous=NORMAL (safe under WAL) + larger cache (currently 2MB) in db/index.ts. (c) performance_snapshots.result GSC blobs (~160KB) parsed wholesale per read — same pattern as recommendation_sets, less acute (point reads). (d) Intelligence slices assembled sequentially (no Promise.all) — parallelize when slice count/cost grows. Progress 2026-06-26: added WAL-safe synchronous=NORMAL and cache_size=-20000 PRAGMAs; capped lost-visibility row/key read models to the top 200 rows while keeping count semantics uncapped; added discovered_queries pruning from both the rank scheduler and global data-retention cron with one-year retention while preserving the top 200 lost-visibility rows. DONE 2026-06-26: parallelized workspace intelligence slice assembly while preserving requested-order assignment and per-slice failure isolation; moved competitor performance snapshot list/latest selection onto SQLite JSON projection so list views and latest-candidate matching do not parse every stored result blob. Deferred recommendation captured as simp-delta-0626-pagespeed-snapshot-projection: PageSpeed/admin-chat/site-health/page-profile still need a dedicated summary/page-score projection rather than full result blob reads.
